races.name: Human
races.categoryDescription: Creator of civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, and social order.
races.description: Humans are highly adaptable and found in nearly every environment across the world. They are known for their cultural diversity, short generational cycles, and unmatched versatility among mortal races.
races.pantheonInfluence: Nebyrr shapes this lineage through civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.canonTraits: Resourceful, Skillful, Versatile (Player’s Handbook).
races.belavadosAbilities: Adaptive Heritage: gains a flexible proficiency, language, or cultural contact chosen at character creation.; Lineage Bridge: advantage or narrative leverage when mediating between ancestry-linked cultures.; Signature Expression: manifests a small but clear ancestral gift chosen with the DM to match the lineage.; Creator's Mark: Nebyrr leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.dmHook: Use Human NPCs to show how Nebyrr's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.name: Human
sheet.analogue: Human - Direct SRD 5.1 analogue
sheet.skillsText: Any two skills tied to background or culture
sheet.weaponsText: Any simple or martial weapon by class; versatile picks include longsword, spear, light crossbow
sheet.traitsText: Adaptable culture, extra language, broad training
sheet.personality: Protective, adaptable, and culturally distinctive.
sheet.flaw: Can overcommit to the role expected by ancestry or community.
sheet.bond: Bound to clan, kin, homeland, or a chosen found-family.
sheet.concept: Humans are highly adaptable and found in nearly every environment across the world. They are known for their cultural diversity, short generational cycles, and unmatched versatility among mortal races.

races.name: Half-Elf
races.categoryDescription: Creator of civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, and social order.
races.description: Half-elves are dual-natured beings born of human and elven lineage, often balancing ambition with longevity. They tend to struggle with identity but excel at diplomacy, adaptability, and bridging cultures.
races.pantheonInfluence: Nebyrr shapes this lineage through civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.canonTraits: +2 Charisma, +1 to Two Other Ability Scores, Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Skill Versatility (Basic Rules (2014)).
races.belavadosAbilities: Adaptive Heritage: gains a flexible proficiency, language, or cultural contact chosen at character creation.; Lineage Bridge: advantage or narrative leverage when mediating between ancestry-linked cultures.; Signature Expression: manifests a small but clear ancestral gift chosen with the DM to match the lineage.; Creator's Mark: Nebyrr leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.dmHook: Use Half-Elf NPCs to show how Nebyrr's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.name: Half-Elf
sheet.analogue: Half-Elf - Direct SRD 5.1 analogue
sheet.skillsText: Persuasion plus any one of Insight, Perception, or Performance
sheet.traitsText: Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Skill Versatility
sheet.personality: Protective, adaptable, and culturally distinctive.
sheet.flaw: Can overcommit to the role expected by ancestry or community.
sheet.bond: Bound to clan, kin, homeland, or a chosen found-family.
sheet.concept: Half-elves are dual-natured beings born of human and elven lineage, often balancing ambition with longevity. They tend to struggle with identity but excel at diplomacy, adaptability, and bridging cultures.

races.name: Half-Orc
races.categoryDescription: Creator of civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, and social order.
races.description: Half-orcs are powerful hybrids of human and orc ancestry, often embodying both resilience and emotional intensity. They are frequently underestimated but possess remarkable endurance and survival instincts.
races.pantheonInfluence: Nebyrr shapes this lineage through civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.canonTraits: +2 Strength, +1 Constitution, Darkvision, Menacing, Relentless Endurance, Savage Attacks (Basic Rules (2014)).
races.belavadosAbilities: Adaptive Heritage: gains a flexible proficiency, language, or cultural contact chosen at character creation.; Lineage Bridge: advantage or narrative leverage when mediating between ancestry-linked cultures.; Signature Expression: manifests a small but clear ancestral gift chosen with the DM to match the lineage.; Creator's Mark: Nebyrr leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.dmHook: Use Half-Orc NPCs to show how Nebyrr's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.name: Half-Orc
sheet.analogue: Half-Orc - Direct SRD 5.1 analogue
sheet.traitsText: Darkvision, Menacing, Relentless Endurance, Savage Attacks
sheet.personality: Alert, direct, and quick to evaluate threats and resources.
sheet.flaw: May reduce social conflicts to dominance, territory, or survival.
sheet.bond: Protects hunting grounds, clan trails, or a sacred natural cycle.
sheet.concept: Half-orcs are powerful hybrids of human and orc ancestry, often embodying both resilience and emotional intensity. They are frequently underestimated but possess remarkable endurance and survival instincts.

races.name: Khoravar
races.categoryDescription: Creator of civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, and social order.
races.description: Khoravar are a refined mixed-heritage people often shaped by structured societies and cosmopolitan regions. They are known for blending martial discipline with intellectual or civic achievement.
races.pantheonInfluence: Nebyrr shapes this lineage through civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.canonTraits: Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Fey Gift, Lethargy Resilience, Skill Versatility (Eberron: Forge of the Artificer).
races.belavadosAbilities: Adaptive Heritage: gains a flexible proficiency, language, or cultural contact chosen at character creation.; Lineage Bridge: advantage or narrative leverage when mediating between ancestry-linked cultures.; Signature Expression: manifests a small but clear ancestral gift chosen with the DM to match the lineage.; Creator's Mark: Nebyrr leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.dmHook: Use Khoravar NPCs to show how Nebyrr's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.name: Khoravar
sheet.analogue: Half-Elf - Homebrew label using Half-Elf analogue
sheet.skillsText: Persuasion plus any one of Insight, Perception, or Performance
sheet.traitsText: Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Skill Versatility
sheet.personality: Disciplined, duty-driven, and respectful of tested command structures.
sheet.flaw: Can be rigid, proud, or slow to compromise.
sheet.bond: Serves a banner, oath, unit, ancestor, or sworn cause.
sheet.concept: Khoravar are a refined mixed-heritage people often shaped by structured societies and cosmopolitan regions. They are known for blending martial discipline with intellectual or civic achievement.

races.name: Umbral Human
races.categoryDescription: Creator of civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, and social order.
races.description: Umbral humans are touched by shadowed ancestry or planar darkness, giving them an eerie presence. They often possess heightened perception in low light and a natural affinity for secrecy or introspection.
races.pantheonInfluence: Nebyrr shapes this lineage through civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.canonTraits: +2 to One Ability Score, +1 to One Other Ability Score, Darkvision, Dark Infusion, Fade Away (Grim Hollow: Player Pack).
races.belavadosAbilities: Adaptive Heritage: gains a flexible proficiency, language, or cultural contact chosen at character creation.; Lineage Bridge: advantage or narrative leverage when mediating between ancestry-linked cultures.; Shadow Familiarity: sees, hides, or navigates better in dim light, ruins, and underworld spaces.; Creator's Mark: Nebyrr leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, inheritance, and social order.
races.dmHook: Use Umbral Human NPCs to show how Nebyrr's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.traitsText: Adaptable culture, extra language, broad training
sheet.personality: Quiet, observant, and comfortable acting from the edges of society.
sheet.flaw: May default to secrecy even when trust would solve the problem.
sheet.bond: Protects the few people who know the character behind the mask.

races.name: Elf (Core Elves)
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Elves are long-lived, graceful beings deeply attuned to magic, nature, and art. Their societies often value tradition, beauty, and refined mastery of specialized skills. They are broadly divided across many cultural and biological lineages throughout the worlds of the multiverse.
races.pantheonInfluence: Sigrananna shapes this lineage through forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.canonTraits: Darkvision, Elven Lineage, Fey Ancestry, Keen Senses, Trance (Player’s Handbook).
races.belavadosAbilities: Fey Memory: resistant to charm-like influence and skilled at trance, dream, or memory rituals.; Graceful Hunter: heightened perception and silent movement in wilderness or moonlit terrain.; Signature Expression: manifests a small but clear ancestral gift chosen with the DM to match the lineage.; Creator's Mark: Sigrananna leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.dmHook: Use Elf (Core Elves) NPCs to show how Sigrananna's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.name: Elf
sheet.analogue: Elf - Direct SRD 5.1 analogue
sheet.traitsText: Darkvision, Keen Senses, Fey Ancestry, Trance
sheet.personality: Protective, adaptable, and culturally distinctive.
sheet.flaw: Can overcommit to the role expected by ancestry or community.
sheet.bond: Bound to clan, kin, homeland, or a chosen found-family.
sheet.concept: Elves are long-lived, graceful beings deeply attuned to magic, nature, and art. Their societies often value tradition, beauty, and refined mastery of specialized skills.

races.name: High Elf
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Scholarly and magically inclined elves who emphasize arcane study, civilization, and refined tradition. Often associated with structured societies and formal magical education.
races.pantheonInfluence: Sigrananna shapes this lineage through forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.canonTraits: Elf lineage, Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Keen Senses, Trance, Arcane Cantrip, Scholarly Tradition (Belavadös lineage supplement + base D&D lineage pattern).
races.belavadosAbilities: Fey Memory: resistant to charm-like influence and skilled at trance, dream, or memory rituals.; Graceful Hunter: heightened perception and silent movement in wilderness or moonlit terrain.; Animal Gift: expresses a signature animal sense, movement, claw, horn, memory, or social instinct.; Creator's Mark: Sigrananna leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.dmHook: Use High Elf NPCs to show how Sigrananna's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Drow (Underdark Elf)
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Dark-reflection elves who dwell beneath the surface world in vast subterranean realms. Often shaped by harsh theocratic societies, they are known for their affinity with shadow, spider symbolism, and survival in the Underdark.
races.pantheonInfluence: Sigrananna shapes this lineage through forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.canonTraits: Elf lineage, Superior Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Keen Senses, Trance, Drow Magic, Underdark Adaptation (closest visible source pattern: Drow (Underdark Elf); Belavadös lineage supplement + base D&D lineage pattern).

races.name: Eladrin
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Fey-touched elves whose emotions and magical expressions shift with the seasons or emotional states. They are closely tied to the Feywild and its ever-changing nature.
races.pantheonInfluence: Sigrananna shapes this lineage through forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.canonTraits: Elf, Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Fey Step, Keen Senses, Trance (Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse).
races.belavadosAbilities: Fey Memory: resistant to charm-like influence and skilled at trance, dream, or memory rituals.; Graceful Hunter: heightened perception and silent movement in wilderness or moonlit terrain.; Aquatic Adaptation: swims naturally and reads water pressure, currents, or shoreline danger.; Creator's Mark: Sigrananna leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.dmHook: Use Eladrin NPCs to show how Sigrananna's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.name: Eladrin
sheet.analogue: Eladrin - Published 5e analogue or common 5e conversion
sheet.traitsText: Fey Step, seasonal emotional magic, Fey Ancestry
sheet.personality: Protective, adaptable, and culturally distinctive.
sheet.flaw: Can overcommit to the role expected by ancestry or community.
sheet.bond: Bound to clan, kin, homeland, or a chosen found-family.
sheet.concept: Eladrin are seasonally attuned elves whose emotions and abilities shift with the cycles of nature. Their personalities and magic often reflect the dominant emotional season they embody.

races.name: Astral Elf
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Originating from the Astral Plane or cosmic realms beyond mortal understanding, astral elves carry a starborne perspective and innate connection to psychic and stellar magic.
races.pantheonInfluence: Sigrananna shapes this lineage through forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.canonTraits: Elf, Astral Fire, Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Keen Senses, Starlight Step, Astral Trance (Spelljammer: Adventures in Space).
races.belavadosAbilities: Fey Memory: resistant to charm-like influence and skilled at trance, dream, or memory rituals.; Graceful Hunter: heightened perception and silent movement in wilderness or moonlit terrain.; Mindborne Talent: communicates, defends, or senses through psychic discipline.; Creator's Mark: Sigrananna leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.dmHook: Use Astral Elf NPCs to show how Sigrananna's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.name: Astral Elf
sheet.analogue: Astral Elf - Spelljammer UA / later 5e analogue
sheet.traitsText: Astral Fire, Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Trance, starborne awareness
sheet.personality: Hopeful, composed, and driven by a sense of higher purpose.
sheet.flaw: May carry guilt when reality fails to match ideals.
sheet.bond: Serves a vision, prophecy, temple, or person in need of protection.
sheet.concept: Astral elves originate from celestial or planar realms beyond the Material Plane, often carrying a cosmic perspective. They tend to be detached from mortal concerns and possess an innate connection to starborne magic.

races.name: Sea Elf
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Aquatic elves adapted to life beneath the waves. They thrive in oceanic environments, maintaining deep cultural ties to marine ecosystems, reef civilizations, and seafaring alliances.
races.pantheonInfluence: Sigrananna shapes this lineage through forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.canonTraits: Elf, Child of the Sea, Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Friend of the Sea, Keen Senses, Trance (Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse).
races.belavadosAbilities: Fey Memory: resistant to charm-like influence and skilled at trance, dream, or memory rituals.; Graceful Hunter: heightened perception and silent movement in wilderness or moonlit terrain.; Aquatic Adaptation: swims naturally and reads water pressure, currents, or shoreline danger.; Creator's Mark: Sigrananna leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.dmHook: Use Sea Elf NPCs to show how Sigrananna's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.bond: Bound to clan, kin, homeland, or a chosen found-family.
sheet.concept: Sea elves are aquatic descendants of elvenkind adapted to oceanic life and underwater civilizations. They are natural swimmers with deep cultural ties to marine ecosystems and seafaring traditions.

races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Shadow-bound elves tied to bleak or shadowy realms, often serving powerful entities of the Shadowfell. They are hardened, disciplined, and resilient due to their exposure to despair and entropy.
races.pantheonInfluence: Sigrananna shapes this lineage through forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.canonTraits: Elf, Blessing of the Raven Queen, Darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Keen Senses, Necrotic Resistance, Trance (Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse).

races.name: Kaluseban
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Elegant Mana-Bound Humanoids of Ley Lines, Nature, Moonlight, and Shadow. Lore: Kalusebans are an elegant and enigmatic race of magical humanoids . They are tall and slender, with graceful features and skin tones that reflect their bloodline, ranging from pale ivory to deep sapphire or earthy greens and browns. Their eyes often glow faintly, carrying hues tied to their magical affinity, and their hair can shimmer, float slightly, or reflect celestial or natural motifs. Kalusebans carry an aura of otherworldly presence, appearing almost ethereal as if the ambient magic bends subtly around them. They thrive in environments rich with mana, nature, or celestial energy, and their forms are highly sensitive to ritual, lunar phases, and environmental harmony. Kalusebans require periodic exposure to magical energy, whether through ley lines, enchanted sites, or ceremonial practices, to maintain their vitality and arcane strength. They bond strongly with natural environments, finding balance and clarity among forests, rivers, or sacred groves. Rituals, music, meditation, or alignment with celestial bodies enhance their mental and emotional equilibrium. Deprivation from magic, polluted areas, or urban confines can weaken their vitality, induce restlessness, or diminish their magical attunement. They also maintain basic needs like nutrition, hydration, and hygiene, often observing these through symbolic or ceremonial acts that reinforce cultural traditions. Kalusebans have abilities tied to their bloodline and affinity. They can manipulate natural or arcane energies, commune with flora and fauna, sense ley lines or currents of magical energy, and subtly influence the environment around them.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Sigrananna. Sigrananna is the primary creator of the Kaluseban, shaping their ley-line grace, moonlit nature, and shadow-touched elegance from wilderness beauty and lunar power. Kaluseban bodies are often described as living ley-script: skin, eyes, hair, and bloodline traits reveal how moonlight, forest memory, and ambient magic wrote themselves into mortal form. Secondary Divine Pressures. Eirzunet haunts umbral bloodlines, forbidden transformations, cursecraft, and twilight bargains. Bastveig influences Tideborn Kaluseban who listen to reef, river, mist, and rain as extensions of living water memory. Horundar shapes Stonebound and storm-touched lines through pressure, earthquake, weather, and elemental endurance. Raeshkul is honored by arcane scholars who map ley currents as thought-patterns, while Thalunesh appears in dream rites and wandering moonlit pilgrimages. Bloodline Influence Notes. Arcane Kaluseban tend to frame Raeshkul and Sigrananna as paired forces: pattern and beauty. Starborne lines often add Sokhivar's prophecy and distant light. Wildroot lines belong most clearly to Sigrananna's forests, while Tideborn lines blend Sigrananna with Bastveig. Stonebound lines may honor Horundar or Anubaldir depending on whether they read stone as force or burden. Umbral Kaluseban are the most likely to carry Eirzunet's dangerous inheritance. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Moonroot Vigil is a night-long meditation at a ley crossing, where a Kaluseban listens for which god's hunger is nearest to their bloodline. Polluting a ley line, binding a grove without consent, or using beauty to hide predation are serious taboos. Ichor-touched Kaluseban may cast two shadows, glow under moonless skies, hear roots speaking in old languages, or develop silver-black veins that flare during eclipses.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Mana-Bound Vitality. You require periodic exposure to magical energy, sacred natural sites, ley lines, celestial light, ritual practice, or enchanted environments to remain fully balanced. Long-term deprivation from magic may cause exhaustion, restlessness, or diminished spellcasting at the DM's discretion.; Leyline Sense. You gain proficiency in Arcana or Nature. You can sense whether a place has strong natural, celestial, arcane, shadow, aquatic, or earthen magical resonance after spending 1 minute in quiet focus.; Subtle Channeling. You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid, cleric, or wizard spell list. Wisdom, Intelligence, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it, chosen when you select this race.; Ritual Poise. You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed by illusions, dream effects, or magic that distorts your perception of reality.; Bloodline Affinity. Choose one Kaluseban bloodline. You gain the bloodline gift listed for it.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language tied to your home culture, bloodline, or magical tradition. Best Classes: Druid, Wizard, Cleric, Warlock, Bard, Ranger, Sorcerer, or any ritual-focused class.
races.dmHook: Use Kaluseban NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Geisamahi
races.categoryDescription: Divine smith and architect of invention, craft, metalwork, and underground halls.
races.description: Small Ferretfolk Tinkerers, Scouts, and Clever Problem-Solvers. Bloodlines / Lineages: Geisamahi (ferretfolk tinkerers, scouts, awakened mechanists, adaptive builders) Lore: The Geisamahis are small, agile creatures . They resemble large ferrets or stoats but walk upright and possess opposable thumbs, allowing them to manipulate objects with precision. Their bodies are wiry and compact, built for speed, nimbleness, and the ability to navigate tight spaces, while their elongated snouts, expressive eyes, and twitching whiskers reflect their alertness and curiosity. Fur varies in earthy browns, fiery oranges, and snowy whites, often with individualized patterns, and many adorn themselves with small tools, belts, and pouches containing gadgets or relics. They are highly adaptable, thriving in forests, ruins, urban alleys, or other cluttered or complex environments, and they maintain mental sharpness through exploration, invention, and playful problem-solving. Geisamahis have heightened senses and dexterity, allowing them to detect hidden passages, traps, or valuable items that others might miss. They are quick learners and excel at crafting devices, tools, and improvised mechanisms to aid in survival or exploration. Their movements are fluid and acrobatic, capable of extended jumps, climbing, and rapid dashes, enabling them to evade danger or reach otherwise inaccessible areas. They possess a natural instinct for teamwork, often coordinating with kin or allies in subtle, synchronized ways. Their energy and vitality are maintained through frequent rest, light, energy-rich meals, and exposure to natural environments. Geisamahis exhibit keen awareness of their surroundings, sensing traps, subtle hazards, or changes in terrain that might threaten their safety. Their small stature and nimble frame allow them to slip unnoticed through gaps or under cover, and their reflexes enable rapid reactions to threats.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Marduthor. Marduthor is the primary creator of the Geisamahi, blessing their small ferretfolk hands with tinkering genius, scouting precision, clever repair instincts, and tool-minded problem solving. Many Geisamahi believe the first tool was not a hammer, but a hiding place: invention began when small people learned to survive inside the cracks of large empires. Secondary Divine Pressures. Setrimir is revered by inventors who build devices whose purpose may outgrow their maker. Oskar'enlil studies Geisamahi adaptability as evidence that civilization can evolve through pressure, constraint, and structured experimentation. Nebyrr influences guild charters, apprenticeship contracts, and workshop inheritance, while Ishtanora is honored in burrow-homes where practical inventions protect family, food stores, and community comfort. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The First Fixed Thing is the rite of adulthood: a young Geisamahi must repair or improve something useful to the whole burrow. The Gearwake is a memorial where a dead inventor's unfinished designs are distributed to friends, rivals, and students so no idea dies alone. The greatest taboo is building a machine that consumes people while pretending it only serves them, because that repeats the gods' own predatory logic. Ichor-touched Geisamahi may develop metallic whiskers, clock-tick heartbeats, sparks in their footprints, or an instinctive sense for cursed mechanisms. DM Hooks. Use their divine influence for tinker guilds, sewer scouts, arcane locksmiths, living machines, rival workshops, god-powered engines, or small inventors trying to keep Setrimir's stolen fire out of exploitative hands.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Intelligence or Wisdom score increases by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Small.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet. You also have a climbing speed of 30 feet.; Nimble Body. You can move through the space of any creature that is at least one size larger than you. Squeezing through spaces large enough for a Small creature costs you no extra movement.; Keen Whiskers and Nose. You gain proficiency in Perception or Investigation. You have advantage on checks made to locate hidden passages, traps, small objects, relics, food, scents, tracks, or recent disturbances in cluttered terrain.; Tinker-Claws. You gain proficiency with thieves' tools, tinkerer's tools, or one artisan's tool of your choice. During a short rest, you may improvise a tiny device, wedge, hook, noisemaker, trip-cord, patch, or simple mechanism from scavenged materials. The device lasts until your next long rest or until used.; Acrobatic Dash. As a bonus action, you may Dash, Disengage, or climb without provoking opportunity attacks until the end of your turn. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.; Coordinated Instinct. When an ally within 5 feet of you makes an ability check using a skill or tool you are proficient with, you may use your reaction to grant that ally a +1d4 bonus to the roll. You can use this trait once per short or long rest.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language of your choice. Best Classes: Rogue, Artificer, Ranger, Bard, Monk, Fighter, or any scout/tinker role.

sheet.bond: Bound to ancestry, found family, creator-god tradition, or faction obligation.
sheet.concept: Small Ferretfolk Tinkerers, Scouts, and Clever Problem-Solvers. Bloodlines / Lineages: Geisamahi (ferretfolk tinkerers, scouts, awakened mechanists, adaptive builders) Lore: The Geisamahis are small, agile creatures . They resemble large ferrets or stoats but walk upright and possess opposable thumbs, allowing them to manipulate objects with precision. Their bodies are wiry and compact, built for speed, nimbleness, and the ability to navigate tight spaces, while their elongated snouts, expressive eyes, and twitching whiskers reflect their alertness and curiosity. Fur varies in earthy browns, fiery oranges, and snowy whites, often with individualized patterns, and many adorn themselves with small tools, belts, and pouches containing gadgets or relics. They are highly adaptable, thriving in forests, ruins, urban alleys, or other cluttered or complex environments, and they maintain mental sharpness through exploration, invention, and playful problem-solving. Geisamahis have heightened senses and dexterity, allowing them to detect hidden passages, traps, or valuable items that others might miss. They are quick learners and excel at crafting devices, tools, and improvised mechanisms to aid in survival or exploration. Their movements are fluid and acrobatic, capable of extended jumps, climbing, and rapid dashes, enabling them to evade danger or reach otherwise inaccessible areas. They possess a natural instinct for teamwork, often coordinating with kin or allies in subtle, synchronized ways. Their energy and vitality are maintained through frequent rest, light, energy-rich meals, and exposure to natural environments. Geisamahis exhibit keen awareness of their surroundings, sensing traps, subtle hazards, or changes in terrain that might threaten their safety. Their small stature and nimble frame allow them to slip unnoticed through gaps or under cover, and their reflexes enable rapid reactions to threats.

races.name: Dragonborn
races.categoryDescription: Creator of thunder-blooded sovereign races tied to ancient power and domination.
races.description: Dragonborn are draconic humanoids born of ancient dragon lineage, elemental force, dynastic memory, and divine sovereignty. In Belavadös, a Dragonborn may carry traditional chromatic, metallic, or gem ancestry while also belonging to a Belavadös dragon-parentage lineage.
races.pantheonInfluence: Valkhamesh and Nebyrr created the Dragonborn as scaled heirs of dragon parentage, sovereignty, storm-born dominion, lineage law, and dynastic memory. Creator God: Valkhamesh. Valkhamesh is honored as the creator of draconic bloodlines, sovereignty, storms, and ancient dominion. Dragonborn are not treated as a single uniform people; each lineage carries the mark of its dragon parentage, but the first divine ignition of draconic ancestry is traced to Valkhamesh's storm-bound authority. Scales, breath, wings, claws, and lineage instincts are read as living proof that ancient dominion still moves through mortal bodies. Secondary Divine Pressures. Nebyrr shapes Dragonborn inheritance, marriage law, dynasty records, oaths of bloodline legitimacy, and the legal status of mixed parentage. Enkirael influences war-clutches, conquest cults, honor-duels, and militant lineages that measure worth through victory. Horundar pulls oceanic, storm, volcanic, and pressure-bound lineages toward elemental extremes. Marduthor is revered by forge-scale clans that turn draconic anatomy into architecture, armor, and empire-building craft. Sigrananna influences forest, moonlit, green, and fey-adjacent dragonborn lineages that guard sacred groves or ecological pacts. Nefarokir governs funerary rites for dragonborn whose bloodlines remember the dead too strongly. Lineage Divine Use. A Dragonborn lineage can keep Valkhamesh as its creator-god while adding a patron pressure based on the parent dragons. Air, storm, and weather lineages often invoke Valkhamesh and Horundar together. Mountain, stone, crystal, and forge lineages often add Marduthor or Anubaldir. Ocean, tide, river, and reef lineages often add Bastveig or Horundar. Shadow, light, dream, and illusion lineages may add Raeshkul, Nephthysra, or Nefarokir depending on whether the culture frames the power as revelation, charisma, or liminal haunting. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Storm-Scale Naming is the most common birth rite: a hatchling or newly acknowledged Dragonborn is presented beneath open sky, near flame, stone, sea, or shadow according to lineage, and elders name the first dragon virtue seen in the child. The Bloodline Ledger is maintained by priests of Nebyrr so inheritance does not become mere conquest. A common taboo forbids claiming another Dragonborn's parentage without proof, because false bloodline claims are considered theft from Valkhamesh and fraud against Nebyrr. Ichor signs include storm-bright veins beneath the scales, breath that sparks before strong emotion, scale patterns resembling old territorial maps, and dreams of ancient wings over the world.
races.canonTraits: Dragon Ancestry, Breath Weapon, Damage Resistance, Darkvision, Draconic Flight. Combined Ability Rule: A Belavadös Dragonborn gains the traditional Dragonborn trait package and the parentage features of its Belavadös dragon lineage. The breath, resistance, perception, and flight expressions remain active as standard draconic inheritance; the parentage features add bloodline-specific magic, senses, movement, survival, social, and elemental gifts. Belavadös Dragonborn Trait Framework: Ability Score Increase: Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type: You are a Humanoid with draconic ancestry.; Size: You are Medium.; Speed: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Darkvision: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.; Draconic Body: You have scaled skin, claws, a powerful tail, and facial ridges tied to your bloodline. Your claws are natural weapons that deal 1d6 slashing damage. You also have advantage on ability checks or saving throws made to keep your balance.; Winged Heritage: Your wings are part of your natural body. When you fall and are not incapacitated, you may use your reaction to reduce falling damage by an amount equal to five times your level. Starting at 5th level, you may fly for 1 minute at a flying speed equal to your walking speed. Once you use this flight, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.; Elemental Channeling: Choose one Dragonborn lineage. You gain its parentage ability package. When a lineage ability requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier, chosen when you select this race.; Ancestral Presence: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Arcana, History, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, or Religion.; Languages: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic. Belavadös Dragon-Parentage Lineages: Noitzideq (Air and Weather): parent dragons Asaafi + Sauqonsex, with Boisein storm influence; inherited theme Sky, weather, air/wind, storm movement, and healing currents.; profile Neutral Coordinator; scores Altruism 2000, Lawfulness 1650, Cooperation 2200, Honor 1850; Saelftum (Volcanic Flame): parent dragons Erymasos + Oreilhya, with Zrom elemental pressure; inherited theme Fire, heat, molten resilience, temperature control, and volcanic endurance.; profile Lawful Defender; scores Altruism 1650, Lawfulness 2050, Cooperation 1700, Honor 1750; Jazwed (Lava and Crystal): parent dragons Erymasos + Nenshexeq + Zrom; inherited theme Fire, stone, crystal force, elemental shaping, and defensive engineering.; profile Lawful Enforcer; scores Altruism 1600, Lawfulness 2100, Cooperation 1550, Honor 1700; Khoalaros (Tide and Ocean): parent dragons Philous + Xuqhuath + Yichah; inherited theme Sea control, water shaping, currents, swimming grace, aquatic communication, and healing water.; profile Neutral Guardian; scores Altruism 2050, Lawfulness 1500, Cooperation 2100, Honor 2000; Gauksneth (Desert Sand and Hidden Water): parent dragons Innoxia + Nenshexeq + Yichah/Xuqhuath; inherited theme Earth shaping, stone sense, sand survival, hidden-water detection, and oasis stewardship.; profile Lawful Coordinator; scores Altruism 1700, Lawfulness 2200, Cooperation 2050, Honor 1600; Tuorszom (Stone and Mountain): parent dragons Nenshexeq + Innoxia + Aloic; inherited theme Stone control, earth shaping, metal/mineral awareness, tremor sense, and mountain endurance.; profile Lawful Sentinel; scores Altruism 1800, Lawfulness 2300, Cooperation 1600, Honor 2250; Taudinax (Shadow and Light): parent dragons Ekhoq + Pteris + Xacuacsih/Thusekha, with Kiczi illusion influence; inherited theme Shadow manipulation, darkness, illusion, tactical concealment, and controlled light.; profile Neutral Strategist; scores Altruism 1450, Lawfulness 1300, Cooperation 1450, Honor 1350; Gouxelq (Metal and Crystal): parent dragons Aloic + Nenshexeq + Zrom; inherited theme Metal control, stone/crystal shaping, elemental structure, and crafted barriers.; profile Lawful Coordinator; scores Altruism 1750, Lawfulness 2300, Cooperation 2250, Honor 1700; Vautaces (Radiance and Foresight): parent dragons Thusekha + Xacuacsih + Mumh + Lunngha/Eecors + Chiellieds; inherited theme Light control, radiant protection, perception, foresight, time-sense, and emotional insight.; profile Lawful Arbiter; scores Altruism 1900, Lawfulness 2150, Cooperation 1550, Honor 2300.
races.dmHook: Use Dragonborn NPCs for disputed heirs, storm-sovereign cults, dragon-parentage trials, bloodline fraud, conquest marriages, ancient dragon relics, forbidden lineage experiments, and descendants trying to turn dominion into protection.

races.name: Aburata
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of crossroads, curses, planar mysteries, and supernatural bloodlines.
races.description: Infernal Dual-Form Equinefolk of Ambition, Hellfire, and Commanding Presence. Lore: The Aburatas are a race of infernal beings . They possess two distinct forms that reflect their dual nature: a humanoid form and a quadrupedal, equine-inspired form. In humanoid form, Aburatas have polished, taut skin resembling obsidian, molten iron, or amethyst-violet, often etched with glowing sigils or ember-like vein patterns that pulse with their emotions. Their eyes blaze with infernal fire or sink into abyssal voids, and their hair can range from jet black to flame-red, silver-violet, or stark white. Horns emerge in unique shapes such as spiraled, antlered, or blade-like, glowing faintly with infernal energy, while a prehensile tail flickers with embers or faint hellfire, aiding balance and expressive gestures. In quadrupedal form, their humanoid upper bodies transition into powerful horse-like torsos sheathed in scaled textures reminiscent of volcanic stone or blackened steel, with muscular limbs ending in broad hooves that leave glowing, smoldering prints. The tail grows longer and more expressive, capable of grasping objects or assisting in movements, while the air around them carries heat and shadow, enhancing their imposing presence. Their voices are resonant and hypnotic, capable of commanding attention or inspiring unease. Aburatas are sustained by infernal energy rather than ordinary nourishment, drawing vitality from places of power, fire-lit areas, or sites infused with dark influence. They maintain stamina, focus, and resilience through sheer will and magical energy, and their infernal heritage grants them natural resistance to fire, curses, and corruption. Aburatas are highly charismatic, their presence amplifying social influence, persuasion, and intimidation. They exhibit keen dexterity and precision in both combat and manipulation of objects, aided by sharp, clawed hands in humanoid form.
races.pantheonInfluence: Secondary Divine Pressures. Enkirael influences Aburata war-herds and officer-cults, sharpening ambition into conquest, fury, and survival through dominance. Nephthysra influences salons, bloodline courts, and performers who turn charisma, beauty, and emotional command into social weapons. Nebyrr is invoked by noble Aburata houses whenever inheritance, marriage contracts, adoption, or dynastic succession must be legally sealed. Valkhamesh is respected by militant Aburata dynasties that frame command, cavalry, and storm-oath hierarchy as proof that power should flow downward from the strongest will. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Ember Crossroads Rite marks adulthood: an Aburata stands at a literal or symbolic crossroads and swears the first oath they are willing to pay for. The Bridle of the True Name is a private vow used by lovers, rivals, and war leaders to bind trust without surrendering autonomy. Breaking a bargained word is the worst taboo, because Aburata believe Freyseth hears every oath as food. Those strongly touched by divine ichor may develop black-glass veins, smoke-scented breath, or palm sigils that ache near contracts, curses, or imprisoned divine shrines. DM Hooks. Use Aburata pantheon influence to create infernal contract nobles, cavalry cults, oath-breaking fugitives, curse-lenders, or reformers trying to prove ambition does not have to become cruelty. A heroic Aburata can still carry Freyseth's hunger in their blood while choosing to make every bargain protective instead of predatory.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Your Charisma score increases by 2, and your Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution score increases by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid. You also count as a Fiend for prerequisites, spells, magical effects, or items that specifically affect fiendish creatures.; Size. You are Medium in humanoid form. In quadrupedal form, your body is still treated as Medium for normal play unless the DM rules a scene requires special handling.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet in humanoid form and 40 feet in quadrupedal form.; Dual Infernal Form. As a bonus action, you may shift between humanoid form and quadrupedal equine-inspired form. Your worn equipment merges, resizes, or remains worn as appropriate. In quadrupedal form, you cannot use equipment requiring two humanoid legs, but you gain advantage on ability checks made to run, carry, shove, resist being knocked prone, or traverse rough terrain.; Hellforged Resistance. You have resistance to fire damage. You have advantage on saving throws against curses, magical corruption, and effects that would frighten you.; Infernal Presence. You gain proficiency in Intimidation, Persuasion, Deception, or Performance. When you make a Charisma check against a creature that can see your horns, glowing sigils, ember-veins, or hellfire aura, you may add 1d4 to the roll. You can do so a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.; Horns, Tail, and Hooves. Your horns, prehensile tail, claws, or hooves are natural weapons. They deal 1d6 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. In quadrupedal form, your hooves may leave harmless smoldering prints and your carrying capacity counts as one size larger.; Infernal Spark. You know thaumaturgy. At 3rd level, you may cast hellish rebuke once per long rest. At 5th level, you may cast darkness or flaming sphere once per long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Infernal. Best Classes: Warlock, Paladin, Bard, Sorcerer, Fighter, Rogue, or any charismatic commander role.
races.dmHook: Use Aburata NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.concept: Infernal Dual-Form Equinefolk of Ambition, Hellfire, and Commanding Presence. Lore: The Aburatas are a race of infernal beings . They possess two distinct forms that reflect their dual nature: a humanoid form and a quadrupedal, equine-inspired form. In humanoid form, Aburatas have polished, taut skin resembling obsidian, molten iron, or amethyst-violet, often etched with glowing sigils or ember-like vein patterns that pulse with their emotions. Their eyes blaze with infernal fire or sink into abyssal voids, and their hair can range from jet black to flame-red, silver-violet, or stark white. Horns emerge in unique shapes such as spiraled, antlered, or blade-like, glowing faintly with infernal energy, while a prehensile tail flickers with embers or faint hellfire, aiding balance and expressive gestures. In quadrupedal form, their humanoid upper bodies transition into powerful horse-like torsos sheathed in scaled textures reminiscent of volcanic stone or blackened steel, with muscular limbs ending in broad hooves that leave glowing, smoldering prints. The tail grows longer and more expressive, capable of grasping objects or assisting in movements, while the air around them carries heat and shadow, enhancing their imposing presence. Their voices are resonant and hypnotic, capable of commanding attention or inspiring unease. Aburatas are sustained by infernal energy rather than ordinary nourishment, drawing vitality from places of power, fire-lit areas, or sites infused with dark influence. They maintain stamina, focus, and resilience through sheer will and magical energy, and their infernal heritage grants them natural resistance to fire, curses, and corruption. Aburatas are highly charismatic, their presence amplifying social influence, persuasion, and intimidation. They exhibit keen dexterity and precision in both combat and manipulation of objects, aided by sharp, clawed hands in humanoid form.

races.name: Volsarii
races.categoryDescription: Lord of death, the underworld, spirits, and the forgotten dead.
races.description: Four-Armed Ferriers of Souls, Liminal Balance, and Ethereal Wings. Lore: Volsariis are ethereal, multi-limbed beings . They possess four arms as a standard trait, elongated ears that curve upward and nearly resemble horns, and long, sensitive antennae atop their heads that detect changes in the environment, subtle emotions, and spiritual currents. Their skin ranges widely from iridescent shades of deep blues, greens, and storm-gray to luminous pastels, dawn-pinks, or shadowed violets, often streaked or veined with faint magical light, and may carry subtle markings that glow or shift with emotion, spiritual energy, or ambient magic. Their eyes vary from softly glowing gold, silver, and sky-blue to hollow voids, nebula-like patterns, or luminous starlight, reflecting both perception of the physical world and awareness of spirits, dream energy, or hidden currents. Hair flows lightly or appears almost weightless, with colors spanning metallic, pastel, starlit, or shadowed hues, sometimes giving the impression of liquid light, drifting mist, or feather-like strands. They maintain an elegant, deliberate grace in movement, capable of silent terrestrial motion, climbing, gliding, or, in their final life cycle, sprouting vast wings ranging from feathered to gossamer or ethereal forms, allowing them flight and aerial control that expresses both power and precision. Volsariis act as ferriers of the dead for Nyxarion, guiding souls through liminal spaces and maintaining the balance between life and death. They are attuned to both the waking and spiritual realms, able to sense, communicate with, and influence spirits, manage transitions, and preserve memory through subtle manipulation of energy, aura, or pheromones. Their abilities blend ethereal perception, dream- or shadow-influenced magic, and radiant or elemental powers, allowing them to phase through matter, weave illusions, manipulate light, wind, or celestial energies, and project calming, inspiring, or disorienting auras. Rituals, meditation, and communion with sacred or magical sites restore vitality, while harsh, chaotic, or energy-depleted environments can diminish focus and magical resonance. Volsariis draw sustenance primarily from ambient magic, spiritual energy, or dream currents rather than conventional food, though symbolic consumption may accompany ceremonial practices. Their culture emphasizes cycles, reflection, guardianship, guidance, and balance, blending artistic expression, dreamwork, and ritual observances with precise attention to the flow of life, death, and magical currents.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Nefarokir. Nefarokir is the primary creator of the Volsarii, making them four-armed ferriers of souls, liminal balance keepers, spiritual witnesses, and guides through the passage between life and death. Their bodies are understood as living thresholds: two hands may comfort the living while two hands hold the path open for the dead. Secondary Divine Pressures. Nebyrr is invoked by funerary houses, inheritance courts, marriage archives, and lineage keepers who need death to be legally and socially recognized. Sokhivar is sung to by winged Volsarii in their final life-cycle, especially when sunlight, prophecy, music, and aerial freedom guide souls toward the next horizon. Raeshkul influences memory-keepers and psychic mourners who preserve testimony from both sides of death. Eirzunet is feared in shadow passages where a soul can be transformed, hidden, or cursed before it reaches proper accounting. Hathruna is honored by Volsarii who emphasize rebirth, composted grief, and the renewal that follows mourning. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Four Hands Passing is the central funerary rite: one hand closes the eyes, one records the name, one comforts the living, and one gestures toward the unseen road. The Wingwake marks the final life-cycle of a Volsarii whose wings have emerged, often transforming them into honored guides, prophets, or terrifying omens. Abandoning a soul for payment, falsifying a death record, or trapping grief for power are grave taboos. Ichor-touched Volsarii may cast wing-shaped shadows before wings grow, hear clocks stop near the dying, or see black threads connecting the living to unresolved deaths. DM Hooks. Use Volsarii pantheon influence for soul-ferry guilds, industrial death ledgers, funeral courts, prophetic winged elders, memory disputes, escaped souls, or Nefarokir cults trying to turn ferriers into collectors.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Increase your Wisdom or Charisma score by 2, and increase one other ability score by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid. You also count as a Celestial or Fey for prerequisites, spells, magical effects, or items that specifically affect liminal, spirit-touched, or otherworldly beings, chosen when selecting this race.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Four Arms. You have four arms. The secondary pair can hold objects, perform somatic components, open doors, carry tools, or interact with items. They do not grant extra attacks, additional shields, or extra two-handed weapon use unless the DM allows a specific feature to do so.; Antennae and Spirit Senses. You gain proficiency in Insight, Perception, or Religion. You have advantage on checks made to notice emotional shifts, spiritual residue, undead presence, or changes in magical atmosphere.; Ferrier of the Dead. You know spare the dying. At 3rd level, you may cast protection from evil and good once per long rest. At 5th level, you may cast gentle repose or augury once per long rest. Wisdom or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.; Liminal Step. As a bonus action, you may become partially ethereal until the end of your turn. During this movement, you may move through the space of creatures and ignore nonmagical difficult terrain. If you end your turn inside an object, you are shunted to the nearest open space and take force damage equal to your level. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.; Winged Final Cycle. Your wings begin as spiritual, feathered, gossamer, or ethereal structures. When you fall, you may use your reaction to glide and reduce falling damage by five times your level. At 5th level, you may fly for 1 minute at a speed of 30 feet once per long rest.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one language associated with spirits, celestial courts, shadow realms, or ancestral rites. Best Classes: Cleric, Paladin, Warlock, Bard, Monk, Wizard, or any psychopomp/guardian role.
races.dmHook: Use Volsarii NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Gandirosha
races.categoryDescription: Patron of tricksters, shapeshifters, wanderers, dreamers, and magical mischief.
races.description: Luck-Touched Wanderers of Charm, Timing, and Subtle Magic. Lore: Gandiroshas are a race of magical, agile beings . Their presence in Belavadös is defined by charm, intuition, and a deep attunement to both chance and the subtle flows of magic in their environment. They range from petite to tall, standing between three and six feet, with wiry or lithe builds suited to quick movement, climbing, and graceful gestures. Their skin carries a natural, faint glow that can take the form of star-like freckles, soft sigils, or flowing patterns that shift with emotion, magical activity, or sunlight. Hair ranges from honey and chestnut to copper, gold, and jade tones, often styled in flowing waves, curls, or dramatic braids, adorned with charms, beads, or small enchanted trinkets that reflect personal flair and magical presence. Eyes are expressive and varied, including amber, hazel, deep brown, gold, green, or soft blue, capable of subtle luminescence when Gandiroshas focus their magical or intuitive senses. Their clothing blends practicality with artistry, often layered, flowing, or accented with pouches, charms, and symbols, allowing both freedom of movement and the display of individual style. Gandiroshas possess an innate connection to luck, opportunity, and the hidden currents of events around them, allowing them to sense favorable outcomes, detect subtle threats, and influence social or magical situations through intuition, charm, and clever timing. They move with remarkable lightness and agility, slipping past obstacles, navigating crowded spaces, or disappearing briefly from sight with fleeting illusions or environmental blending. Their social skills are heightened, drawing vitality and focus from interaction, shared meals, stories, music, or companionship, while isolation, stress, or chaotic surroundings can diminish their magical acuity. Gandiroshas have finely tuned perception, enabling them to read intentions, uncover hidden treasures, detect traps, and anticipate outcomes with uncanny accuracy. Their subtle magic can influence luck, create brief illusions, manipulate small objects, or enhance group morale through presence and charisma.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Thalunesh. Thalunesh is the primary creator of the Gandirosha, filling them with luck-touched movement, charming mischief, wanderer instincts, and subtle timing magic. Their culture teaches that luck is not random: it is a moving door, and a clever soul learns when to step through before the lock remembers it is closed. Secondary Divine Pressures. Asethyr shapes their revels, road festivals, tribal games, and wild ceremonies where freedom is treated as a sacred survival skill. Nephthysra influences performers, courtiers, and social gamblers who use beauty, emotion, and charm to redirect danger before swords are drawn. Nebyrr is a favorite target and reluctant patron, because Gandirosha often survive by learning lineage laws, travel permits, inheritance loopholes, and marriage customs well enough to bend them. Eirzunet haunts cursed tricks, shadow wagers, false faces, and jokes that become transformations. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Turning Coin Rite is performed before major journeys: a coin, shell, or knucklebone is tossed, but the initiate is judged by how they respond after the result rather than by which side lands upward. The Roadmask Festival allows every participant to lie for one night, but only to reveal a truth by dawn. The greatest taboo is stealing another person's future by tricking them into a bargain they could not understand. Ichor-touched Gandirosha may hear dice before danger, cast the wrong shadow, or briefly see three possible outcomes reflected in mirrors. DM Hooks. Their pantheon ties make Gandirosha ideal as prophetic gamblers, caravan tricksters, oath-law hackers, fey couriers, masked rebels, or cursed comedians whose jokes accidentally open divine doors.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Charisma, Wisdom, or Intelligence score increases by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Small or Medium, chosen when selecting this race.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Fortune Sense. You gain proficiency in Insight, Perception, Sleight of Hand, or Persuasion. You also have advantage on ability checks made to notice traps, hidden valuables, loaded dice, false smiles, or subtle shifts in social mood.; Lucky Timing. When you fail an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, you may roll 1d4 and add it to the result, potentially turning failure into success. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and regain all expended uses after a long rest.; Fleeting Glamour. You know minor illusion. Starting at 3rd level, you may cast disguise self once per long rest. Starting at 5th level, you may cast invisibility once per long rest, targeting only yourself. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.; Lightfoot Agility. You can move through the space of a creature that is at least one size larger than you. You also ignore nonmagical difficult terrain caused by crowds, clutter, loose rubble, market stalls, furniture, or narrow urban spaces.; Communal Spark. During a short rest, if you share food, music, storytelling, games, or friendly conversation with at least one ally, you and one creature who joined you gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language of your choice. Best Classes: Bard, Rogue, Ranger, Monk, Warlock, Sorcerer, or any face/scout role.
races.dmHook: Use Gandirosha NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Air Genasi
races.categoryDescription: Master of storms, seas, earthquakes, floods, and untamed elemental force.
races.description: Born of living wind and storm currents, Air Genasi bodies often feel lighter than they should be and are naturally attuned to altitude and motion. They are frequently seen as distant or restless, as if always partially elsewhere in the sky.
races.pantheonInfluence: Horundar shapes this lineage through storms, oceans, floods, earthquakes, and untamed elemental force.
races.canonTraits: Darkvision, Unending Breath, Lightning Resistance, Mingle with the Wind (Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse).

races.name: Panolima
races.categoryDescription: Master of storms, seas, earthquakes, floods, and untamed elemental force.
races.description: Living Embodiments of Elemental Harmony: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, and Aether. Lore: The Panolimas are beings of elemental harmony . Their bodies are infused with one of the five primordial elemental threads: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, or Aether. Each individual exhibits physical traits reflecting their elemental affinity, from stone-like skin and mossy hair to flowing, water-like forms or luminous, energy-filled auras. They thrive in environments aligned with their element, drawing sustenance, vitality, and energy from landscapes, currents, storms, magma, or ley lines. Their movements, gestures, and even voices often carry the rhythm of their element, and they engage in ritual cleansing or meditation to maintain harmony with their elemental thread. Earth Panolimas have skin patterned like bark or stone, hair resembling moss or soil, and a deep-toned, rumbling voice. They require contact with solid terrain, forests, caves, or mountains to maintain vitality. Their abilities include sensing structural weaknesses, reinforcing the land, and enduring physical hardships with exceptional resilience. They can read terrain, detect mineral-rich soils, and manipulate stone or plant life to shape their surroundings. Fire Panolimas exhibit cracks like magma across their skin, ember-red hair, and glowing eyes. They rely on heat sources, volcanic activity, and exposure to flames to sustain energy. Their bodies radiate warmth and light, and they can manipulate molten materials, summon bursts of flame, and purify areas through fire.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Horundar. Horundar is the primary creator of the Panolima, whose elemental threads of earth, fire, water, air, and aether embody the unstable boundaries of ocean, storm, pressure, and upheaval. Panolima do not merely use elements; they are living negotiations between forces that would otherwise break the world apart. Secondary Divine Pressures. Oskar'enlil studies Panolima balance as a living experiment in civilization, instinct, elemental pressure, and structured survival. Bastveig influences Water Panolima who treat currents and reefs as living memory. Marduthor appears among Fire and Earth Panolima who turn heat, stone, and metal into craft. Sigrananna shapes plant-rich Earth and Air communities through wilderness harmony, while Valkhamesh influences storm-born Panolima who see hierarchy in lightning and pressure. Elemental Influence Notes. Earth Panolima often honor Anubaldir, Marduthor, or Sigrananna depending on whether their culture emphasizes burden, craft, or roots. Fire Panolima may honor Marduthor's forge or Enkirael's battle-flame, creating a major ethical divide. Water Panolima most often answer Horundar and Bastveig together. Air Panolima add Sokhivar for song, prophecy, and sky freedom. Aether Panolima are frequently watched by Raeshkul and Oskar'enlil because their bodies suggest that elements can become thought, law, and living theory. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Fivefold Stillness requires a Panolima to sit within the presence of all five threads and refuse to let any single element dominate the others. Elemental hoarding is taboo: a Panolima who uses their thread to destroy the balance that sustains others is treated as spiritually starving. Ichor-touched Panolima may leak black steam, carry glassy fault-lines in the skin, hear storms before clouds form, or lose control of one element whenever a divine prison nearby hungers.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid. You also count as an Elemental for prerequisites, spells, magical effects, or items that specifically affect elemental creatures.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Elemental Thread. Choose one thread: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, or Aether. Your appearance, diet, movement, rituals, and magical abilities reflect this thread. You gain the corresponding bloodline gift below.; Elemental Communion. You gain proficiency in Nature, Arcana, Survival, or Religion. During a short or long rest in an environment aligned to your element, you may perform communion to gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus + your Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier.; Elemental Cantrip. You know one cantrip tied to your thread: mold earth for Earth, produce flame for Fire, shape water for Water, gust for Air, or prestidigitation for Aether. Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability, chosen when selecting this race.; Harmonic Presence. Your body subtly harmonizes with places aligned to your thread. You can sense hazards, disturbances, and strong elemental currents within 30 feet if you spend 1 minute focusing.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Primordial. Best Classes: Druid, Sorcerer, Cleric, Monk, Ranger, Wizard, Barbarian, or any class reflecting the chosen thread.

sheet.concept: Living Embodiments of Elemental Harmony: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, and Aether. Lore: The Panolimas are beings of elemental harmony . Their bodies are infused with one of the five primordial elemental threads: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, or Aether. Each individual exhibits physical traits reflecting their elemental affinity, from stone-like skin and mossy hair to flowing, water-like forms or luminous, energy-filled auras. They thrive in environments aligned with their element, drawing sustenance, vitality, and energy from landscapes, currents, storms, magma, or ley lines. Their movements, gestures, and even voices often carry the rhythm of their element, and they engage in ritual cleansing or meditation to maintain harmony with their elemental thread. Earth Panolimas have skin patterned like bark or stone, hair resembling moss or soil, and a deep-toned, rumbling voice. They require contact with solid terrain, forests, caves, or mountains to maintain vitality. Their abilities include sensing structural weaknesses, reinforcing the land, and enduring physical hardships with exceptional resilience. They can read terrain, detect mineral-rich soils, and manipulate stone or plant life to shape their surroundings. Fire Panolimas exhibit cracks like magma across their skin, ember-red hair, and glowing eyes. They rely on heat sources, volcanic activity, and exposure to flames to sustain energy. Their bodies radiate warmth and light, and they can manipulate molten materials, summon bursts of flame, and purify areas through fire.

races.name: Kalashtar
races.categoryDescription: Goddess of intellect, strategy, psionic discipline, and higher understanding.
races.description: Bound to quori spirits within the mind, Kalashtar experience layered consciousness and heightened empathy. They are disciplined thinkers who often struggle between dream and reality.
races.pantheonInfluence: Raeshkul shapes this lineage through intellect, psionics, strategy, and higher understanding.
races.canonTraits: Dual Mind, Mental Discipline, Mindlink, Severed from Dreams (Eberron: Forge of the Artificer).
races.belavadosAbilities: Mind Discipline: resists psychic pressure and communicates through thought, dream, or intuition.; Alien Insight: perceives patterns others miss in strategy, probability, or metaphysics.; Mindborne Talent: communicates, defends, or senses through psychic discipline.; Creator's Mark: Raeshkul leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward intellect, psionics, strategy, and higher understanding.
races.dmHook: Use Kalashtar NPCs to show how Raeshkul's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Gwynis
races.categoryDescription: Goddess of intellect, strategy, psionic discipline, and higher understanding.
races.description: Dream-Born Ethereals of Illusion, Memory, and Psychic Insight. Lore: Gwynises are ethereal, dream-born beings . Their appearances range widely, from tall, slender, shadow-touched figures to luminous, starlit forms with flowing, weightless hair and shifting, aurora-like or mist-like skin patterns that reflect thought, emotion, or ambient magical energy. Eyes vary from glowing moons and crystalline orbs to hollow voids or softly luminescent shades, capable of perceiving layers of reality, psychic echoes, or dreamscapes. Their bodies can phase between tangible and ghostly states, trailing wisps, shadowed tendrils, or dreamlike distortions that ripple with their movements, and their fingers and limbs are long, dexterous, and capable of absorbing mana, manipulating subtle forces, or projecting dream energy. Gwynises move with fluid grace and silent precision, able to influence perception, subtly manipulate emotion, or weave ephemeral illusions and constructs from thought, dream, and psychic resonance. They thrive in magical, artistic, or low-light environments, sustaining themselves through dream energy, meditation, ambient magic, or symbolic nourishment, while chaotic or harsh surroundings can diminish focus, well-being, and psychic attunement. Gwynises possess a versatile range of abilities that blend ethereal grace, dreamcraft, and subtle guardianship. They can obscure themselves, manipulate perception, cloud memory, or guide and protect others through psychic insight and dream influence. They are capable of lucid dreamcraft, weaving illusions, threading thought into reality, and sensing hidden truths or intentions in both waking and dream realms. Their physical and metaphysical forms are resilient, enduring environmental extremes while maintaining grace, or phasing through matter and shadows when needed. Some Gwynises manifest ephemeral or surreal adornments, levitating objects, flowing tattoos, or subtle elemental or celestial effects that mirror their inner state. They can influence emotion and perception through presence, pheromones, or vocal resonance, and their aura often bends reality subtly around them, inspiring awe, creativity, or reflection in observers.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Raeshkul. Raeshkul is the primary creator of the Gwynis, shaping their dream-born bodies around psychic insight, memory, illusion, and higher understanding. Their minds are treated as temples without walls: every dream, memory, and false image can become a corridor leading toward strategy, revelation, or danger. Secondary Divine Pressures. Eirzunet haunts Gwynis dream-shadows, forbidden transformations, and nightmares that teach too much. Thalunesh influences wandering dreamers, playful illusions, and the luck of impossible timing. Nefarokir is present when Gwynis preserve the memories of the dead or receive dreams from souls that should have passed on. Sokhivar touches prophetic music, sunlight visions, and winged dream-symbols that guide a seer toward the next horizon. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Mirror-Sleep Rite requires a Gwynis to sleep beside a covered mirror, then uncover it at dawn and describe which part of the self did not return. Memory-braiding is a communal practice where several Gwynis share one important recollection so grief, strategy, or prophecy cannot be erased by a single death. Invading a mind for curiosity alone is a taboo, while erasing grief without consent is treated as spiritual murder. Ichor-touched Gwynis may bleed ink-dark tears, speak in layered voices while dreaming, or carry floating memory motes visible in moonlight. DM Hooks. Their pantheon influence supports psychic diplomats, dream spies, memory archivists, illusionist rebels, nightmare-cursed oracles, and communities targeted by gods who want their secrets back.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma score by 2, and increase one other ability score by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid. You also count as a Fey for prerequisites, spells, magical effects, or items that specifically affect fey creatures.; Size. You are Small or Medium, chosen when selecting this race.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Dreamsight. You have darkvision out to 60 feet. While in dim light, darkness, dreams, magical mist, or an illusion, you can tell whether a creature within 30 feet is awake, asleep, frightened, charmed, or under an illusion, unless that creature is protected by magic that blocks divination.; Lucid Dreamcraft. You know minor illusion. At 3rd level, you may cast silent image once per long rest. At 5th level, you may cast phantasmal force or calm emotions once per long rest. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells, chosen when selecting this race.; Ethereal Flicker. As a reaction when you take damage, you may partially phase. Reduce the damage by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus + the modifier of your chosen spellcasting ability. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.; Memory Threader. You gain proficiency in Insight, Performance, Persuasion, or Stealth. You have advantage on checks made to interpret symbolism, dreams, artistic metaphors, emotional echoes, or psychic impressions.; Dream Rest. You do not sleep in the ordinary way. During a long rest, you enter a lucid meditative dream for 4 hours while remaining dimly aware of your surroundings.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan or one language tied to dream, spirit, or arcane traditions. Best Classes: Bard, Warlock, Sorcerer, Wizard, Cleric, Rogue, or any illusion/psychic support role.
