races.name: Deorbasun
races.categoryDescription: Primordial mother of beasts, fertility, instinct, and natural kinship.
races.description: Majestic Beast-Souled Guardians of Instinct, Honor, and Mystical Territory. Lore: Deorbasuns are majestic. Their physiques range from muscular, feline-like frames to lithe, agile forms with fur, scales, or earth-toned skin, reflecting a deep connection to predatory grace, wilderness instinct, and mystical energy. Heights vary widely, from six to nine feet, and their limbs are long and flexible, ending in retractable claws or talons capable of channeling energy or venom. Eyes glow with intelligence and authority, displaying hues from ember and gold to crystalline blues, greens, or silver, and often carry faint luminescence that responds to magic or emotion. Hair and manes vary from obsidian black, smoldering crimson, and radiant gold to bioluminescent strands, shimmered locks, or ethereal highlights, often adorned with beads, feathers, bones, or gemstones marking achievements, lineage, or ritual significance. Wings, tails, or subtle soul-animal traits enhance both presence and ability, with wings capable of flight or generating gusts, tails delivering venom, radiant energy, or prehensile utility, and faint ethereal markings or patterns tying form to instinct and magic. Deorbasuns possess extraordinary physical and magical abilities that blend predatory skill, shapeshifting versatility, and environmental attunement. They can leap with precision, maneuver through air or terrain with grace, and strike with claws, fangs, or tails imbued with energy or venom. Some can shift subtly to reflect traits of a soul-animal, such as enhanced senses, agility, or elemental affinities, while others maintain a consistently imposing, feline-like or beastly form. Their endurance and stamina are exceptional, allowing them to traverse rugged terrain, hunt, or perform ritualized movement for extended periods. They absorb energy through magical currents, prey, or ambient mana, and their focus and vitality are renewed through hunting, meditation, ritual exposure, or communion with natural and mystical environments. Deorbasuns are highly perceptive, capable of reading subtle environmental cues, sensing magical disturbances, and coordinating with wildlife or allies in ways that reflect both strategic intelligence and instinctual response.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Iskareth. Iskareth is the primary creator of the Deorbasun, shaping their beast-souled bodies from instinct, fertility, natural kinship, and guardian grace. Their soul-animal traits are understood as divine fragments of Iskareth's first wilderness memory: claw, wing, fang, horn, hide, and watchful eye each represent a sacred duty to defend territory without severing kinship with the living world. Secondary Divine Pressures. Anubaldir is honored by territorial guardians who treat endurance and burden as inherited obligations rather than personal virtues. Asethyr influences revels, hunt-feasts, and tribal rites where wilderness freedom becomes sacred survival. Nebyrr appears in boundary law, marriage bonds between guardian clans, and formal oaths that decide who may pass through protected territory. Sigrananna is invoked by moonlit forest Deorbasun, especially those who guard elven groves, old roads, or sacred beasts. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Boundary Vigil sends young Deorbasun to walk the edge of a chosen territory for one full night without speaking, learning where duty ends and domination begins. The First Hunt is not a kill-trial, but a restraint-trial: the initiate must prove they can track, judge, and spare when the land does not require blood. Wasting prey, abandoning a sworn boundary, or hunting only for vanity are deep taboos. Ichor-touched Deorbasun may show glowing paw-print markings, stone-heavy bones, prophetic animal dreams, or a voice that quiets frightened beasts. DM Hooks. Their divine culture supports shrine guardians, border wardens, sacred beast judges, territorial paladins, cursed exiles, or clans divided between Iskareth's kinship and Enkirael's domination. A Deorbasun antagonist does not need to be evil: they may simply believe the party has crossed a boundary the gods themselves once marked.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Your Strength or Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Wisdom or Charisma score increases by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Medium. Many Deorbasuns stand between six and nine feet tall, but your playable size remains Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 35 feet.; Predatory Natural Weapons. Your claws, fangs, talons, horns, or tail are natural weapons. When you hit with an unarmed strike, you may deal slashing, piercing, or bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength or Dexterity modifier.; Soul-Animal Instinct. Choose one instinct when selecting this race: Hunter, Guardian, Winged, Venomtail, or Mystic. Hunter grants proficiency in Stealth or Survival. Guardian grants proficiency in Intimidation or Athletics. Winged grants the Winged Maneuver trait below. Venomtail lets your natural weapon deal poison damage instead of its normal type once per turn. Mystic grants proficiency in Arcana, Nature, or Religion.; Ritual Hunt. Once per long rest, as a bonus action, you mark one creature you can see for 1 minute. The first time each turn you hit the marked creature with an attack, you deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. This damage type matches your natural weapon or a magical energy associated with your soul-animal.; Winged Maneuver. If you chose the Winged instinct, your wings allow brief aerial control. When you jump or fall, you may glide up to 20 feet horizontally and reduce falling damage by an amount equal to five times your level.; Territorial Awareness. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception or Survival) checks made to track creatures, sense intruders, identify disturbed terrain, or notice magical disturbances in your claimed territory or current campsite.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language of your choice. Best Classes: Barbarian, Fighter, Ranger, Druid, Paladin, Monk, or any guardian/hunter role.
races.dmHook: Use Deorbasun NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Triton

races.description: Oceanic guardians adapted to deep-sea environments and coastal defense, Triton are disciplined and duty-bound. They often serve as protectors of aquatic realms.

races.name: Dril'thar
races.categoryDescription: Ancient mother of tides, reefs, hidden waters, and oceanic civilizations.
races.description: Amphibious Mystics of the Living Waters. Lore: The Dril'thar are an ancient amphibious people whose civilizations rise from swamps, flooded jungles, coral reefs, mangrove coasts, subterranean rivers, mist-choked marshes, and sacred rainforests. Across countless regions of the world, they are known as healers, navigators, mystics, hunters, survivalists, and wandering pilgrims capable of thriving where most civilizations would collapse. To outsiders, Dril'thar are often misunderstood as poisonous frogfolk, territorial swamp-creatures, or eerie marsh spirits. In truth, they are one of the most biologically adaptable and spiritually complex peoples in existence. Their philosophy teaches that all things move in cycles: rain becomes river, river becomes sea, decay becomes renewal, and death feeds life anew. Water is not merely an element to the Dril'thar—it is memory, ancestry, spirit, and destiny itself. A spawning pool may serve simultaneously as temple, cemetery, and place of prophecy, for the Dril'thar believe the currents of the world carry the echoes of those who came before.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Bastveig. Bastveig is the primary creator of the Dril'thar, binding amphibious mysticism, reef stewardship, hydromancy, and living water memory into their bodies. Dril'thar spawning pools are not only places of birth, but divine archives: their water is believed to remember every ancestor, every flood, every drowning, and every bargain made with the deep. Secondary Divine Pressures. Thoryn-Rahek appears in mutation myths, especially among lineages whose bodies adapt too quickly or in ways that feel alien even to other Dril'thar. Hathruna shapes marsh, rot, and renewal traditions where decay feeds pools and pools feed new life. Horundar influences ocean, storm, and flood clans who believe catastrophe can purify stagnant civilizations. Nefarokir is present in gravewater rites, where pools serve as cemeteries, memory wells, and thresholds for souls who died in water. Lineage Influence Notes. Grassland Dril'thar often honor Ishtanora for rain-fed survival and hidden wetland gardens. Swamp and marsh lineages commonly invoke Hathruna and Nefarokir together, treating rot and death as two halves of the same sacred current. Rainforest and forest lineages add Sigrananna to Bastveig's rites, while reef and ocean lineages are the most direct inheritors of Bastveig's deep-water civilization. Mutated or unusually colored children may be protected, feared, or consecrated depending on whether the clan reads the sign as Bastveig's blessing or Thoryn-Rahek's echo. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Pool of Names ceremony lets a clan recite its dead into living water so the next generation can dream them. The Tide Confession requires a Dril'thar to speak secrets into flowing water rather than into stone, because stillness traps guilt. Polluting a birth-pool, damming ancestral water without consent, or draining a cemetery-marsh are treated as sacrilege. Ichor-touched Dril'thar may develop black bioluminescent freckles, prophetic mucus patterns, voices that ripple water, or mutations that bloom after divine dreams.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase.; Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type.; You are a Humanoid.; Age.; Dril'thar hatch from gelatinous egg-clutches and rapidly mature during their early metamorphic stages. Most reach adulthood around age 10.; Typical Dril'thar live between 50 and 70 years, though certain deep-water or mystical bloodlines may survive for centuries.; Size.; Dril'thar vary dramatically between lineages and environmental adaptations.; Your size is either Small or Medium (choose when selecting this race).; Speed.; Your base walking speed is 30 feet. Best Classes: Ranger Monk
races.dmHook: Use Dril'thar NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Tamhiogal
races.categoryDescription: Ancient mother of tides, reefs, hidden waters, and oceanic civilizations.
races.description: Aquatic Bettafolk of Hydromancy, Coral Stewardship, and Tidal Spirits. Lore: Tamhiogal are aquatic beings. They resemble betta fish in their coloration, displaying vibrant shades of blues, purples, teals, and crimsons that shift with emotion and magical activity. On land, they possess scaled, graceful humanoid arms and legs, with scales rippling along their cheeks, necks, and collarbones. Their eyes are liquid and reflective, glowing faintly in dim light, and their hair consists of bioluminescent, jellyfish-like strands that can sting mildly when threatened. In water, they have gills along their necks, webbing between fingers and elbows, and fins along the spine, knees, and tail. Their legs are replaced by long, sleek caudal fins, with dorsal and anal fins enhancing maneuverability. Tamhiogal require hydration and tidal energy, which regenerate most efficiently in aquatic environments. They sustain themselves through fish, kelp, and other sea-based foods. Being immersed in water naturally maintains their hygiene and overall well-being. Exposure to polluted or stagnant water causes spiritual and emotional dissonance, while ritual bathing and communion with ocean spirits restore clarity, mood, and magical flow. Tamhiogal possess abilities attuned to both water and land. They can transition smoothly between terrestrial and aquatic forms, allowing fluid movement across both environments.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Bastveig. Bastveig is the primary creator of the Tamhiogal, binding bettafolk hydromancy, coral stewardship, tidal spirits, and aquatic civilization into their blood. Their vivid fins and emotional color-shifts are understood as reef-language inherited from the first living waters, where beauty, warning, courtship, grief, and magic were never separate things. Secondary Divine Pressures. Nephthysra is adored by Tamhiogal courts where beauty, charisma, emotional influence, and rare presence become unsettling divine inheritance. Horundar governs storms, floods, and violent tides, making him both feared and respected among open-ocean clans. Nefarokir is invoked in drowning rites and memorial currents, ensuring souls lost at sea are guided rather than trapped. Ishtanora appears in fishing villages and kelp farms where ocean harvest becomes family survival, while Valkhamesh is honored by royal fleets that claim dominion over harbors and sea-lanes. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Coral Naming lets a Tamhiogal place a fragment of coral, shell, or glass into a communal reef to mark a vow, birth, marriage, or grief. The Duel of Fins is a ritual conflict where beauty and restraint matter as much as victory. Poisoning a reef, enslaving a water spirit, or letting vanity endanger the community are major taboos. Ichor-touched Tamhiogal may develop black-rimmed fins, hypnotic scale patterns, water that curls toward their hands, or songs that make drowned memories surface. DM Hooks. Their pantheon influence supports coral courts, hydromancer diplomats, reef guardians, tempest exiles, beauty cults, drowned royal secrets, or Tamhiogal heroes trying to prove Nephthysra's charisma does not have to become manipulation.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Wisdom or Charisma score increases by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet. In aquatic form, you have a swimming speed of 40 feet.; Amphibious Tidal Form. You can breathe air and water. While fully immersed in water, your legs may shift into a sleek caudal fin, replacing your walking speed with your swimming speed until you return to land.; Hydromancy. You know shape water. At 3rd level, you may cast create or destroy water once per long rest. At 5th level, you may cast lesser restoration or warding bond once per long rest, flavored as healing currents or protective tides. Wisdom or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.; Bioluminescent Tendrils. Your jellyfish-like hair can glow with dim light in a 10-foot radius. When a creature grapples you or hits you with an unarmed strike, you may use your reaction to deal lightning or poison damage equal to your proficiency bonus.; Marine Communion. You can communicate simple ideas with aquatic beasts and spirits. You have advantage on Animal Handling, Nature, or Survival checks involving oceans, rivers, reefs, tides, kelp forests, or aquatic creatures.; Water-Sustained Wellbeing. You require regular hydration, ritual bathing, or immersion to remain healthy and magically balanced. Polluted or stagnant water can cause emotional and spiritual dissonance at the DM's discretion.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Aquan. Best Classes: Druid, Cleric, Sorcerer, Bard, Ranger, Paladin, or any oceanic guardian role.
races.dmHook: Use Tamhiogal NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.concept: Aquatic Bettafolk of Hydromancy, Coral Stewardship, and Tidal Spirits. Lore: Tamhiogal are aquatic beings. They resemble betta fish in their coloration, displaying vibrant shades of blues, purples, teals, and crimsons that shift with emotion and magical activity. On land, they possess scaled, graceful humanoid arms and legs, with scales rippling along their cheeks, necks, and collarbones. Their eyes are liquid and reflective, glowing faintly in dim light, and their hair consists of bioluminescent, jellyfish-like strands that can sting mildly when threatened. In water, they have gills along their necks, webbing between fingers and elbows, and fins along the spine, knees, and tail. Their legs are replaced by long, sleek caudal fins, with dorsal and anal fins enhancing maneuverability. Tamhiogal require hydration and tidal energy, which regenerate most efficiently in aquatic environments. They sustain themselves through fish, kelp, and other sea-based foods. Being immersed in water naturally maintains their hygiene and overall well-being. Exposure to polluted or stagnant water causes spiritual and emotional dissonance, while ritual bathing and communion with ocean spirits restore clarity, mood, and magical flow. Tamhiogal possess abilities attuned to both water and land. They can transition smoothly between terrestrial and aquatic forms, allowing fluid movement across both environments.

races.name: Jaspey
races.categoryDescription: Goddess of seasonal rebirth, decay, fungi, roots, and living wilderness.
races.description: Ability Score Increase. Lore: Tiny fungal raccoon-folk born from ancient symbiotic spores and scavenger spirits, Jaspey are eerie yet lovable creatures that blur the line between woodland fae, living fungus, and grave-born scavenger. Though their appearance often resembles tiny undead raccoons stitched together with moss, mushrooms, and decay, Jaspey are fully living beings with beating hearts, warm emotions, and vibrant cultures. Standing only a few inches tall, Jaspey can sleep inside coat pockets, teacups, backpacks, hollow books, hat brims, or even small tins. Entire hidden settlements may exist unnoticed inside old walls, cemetery gardens, forgotten cupboards, abandoned wagons, or the roots of ancient trees. Jaspey are natural scavengers, herbalists, healers, thieves, potion brewers, and strange little mystics. They collect shiny objects obsessively, form strong emotional bonds with larger companions, and often travel alongside adventuring parties as medics, alchemists, scouts, or magical nuisances.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Hathruna. Hathruna is the primary creator of the Jaspey, blooming tiny fungal raccoon-folk from rot, spores, roots, scavenger spirits, and the cycle of decay into renewal. Jaspey theology teaches that nothing useful is truly trash: what the powerful discard becomes food, medicine, shelter, story, and sometimes rebellion. Secondary Divine Pressures. Ishtanora is a beloved hearth-patron to Jaspey villages because fungal gardens turn decay into comfort, medicine, and communal survival. Nefarokir is respected in grave-gardens where bones, memory, and compost are carefully separated so the dead are not stolen from their proper passage. Thoryn-Rahek is feared in uncontrolled spore blooms, violent mutations, and fungal hungers that no colony can name. Marduthor is honored by scavenger-crafters who turn broken locks, bottlecaps, wire, and discarded tools into practical magic. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The First Find is a rite where a young Jaspey brings home something abandoned and explains how it can serve the colony. The Soft Burial places beloved dead in protected fungal beds, not to erase them, but to let their last gifts become medicine or shelter. Wasting salvage, poisoning a communal spore-bed, or hoarding medicine during sickness are severe taboos. Ichor-touched Jaspey may grow black-edged mushrooms, dream through nearby fungus, or produce spores that briefly reveal hidden decay, lies, or sickness. DM Hooks. Use Jaspey pantheon influence for undercity colonies, fungal healers, sacred scavenger routes, rot-cults, grave-garden disputes, or tiny heroes who know exactly which forgotten scrap can save a doomed settlement.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase.; Your Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Wisdom or Intelligence score increases by 1.; Age.; Jaspey mature rapidly, reaching adulthood around 4 years old. Most live between 25 and 40 years, though ancient fungal sages and colony elders sometimes survive for over a century under ideal conditions.; Alignment.; Jaspey tend toward chaotic or neutral alignments. They value adaptability, freedom, comfort, curiosity, and survival more than laws or rigid traditions.; Most Jaspey are not malicious, though some become selfish scavengers, manipulative tricksters, grave robbers, or unsettling fungal occultists.; Creature Type.; You are a Humanoid.; You are also considered a Plant for any prerequisite, spell, magical effect, or item that specifically affects plant creatures.; Size.; You are Tiny.
races.dmHook: Use Jaspey NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

sheet.name: Jaspey
sheet.analogue: Belavadös compendium race entry
sheet.speedText: 30 ft.; Medium (adjust with DM for aquatic, flying, or unusual bodies)
sheet.weaponsText: Dagger, spear, light crossbow; adjust by class and culture
sheet.traitsText: Belavadös lineage traits
sheet.roles: Any class supported by table context
sheet.personality: Culturally distinctive and shaped by ancestry, deity, and homeland.
sheet.flaw: May feel pressure from lineage expectations, divine influence, faction ties, or survival history.
sheet.bond: Bound to ancestry, found family, creator-god tradition, or faction obligation.
sheet.concept: Ability Score Increase. Lore: Tiny fungal raccoon-folk born from ancient symbiotic spores and scavenger spirits, Jaspey are eerie yet lovable creatures that blur the line between woodland fae, living fungus, and grave-born scavenger. Though their appearance often resembles tiny undead raccoons stitched together with moss, mushrooms, and decay, Jaspey are fully living beings with beating hearts, warm emotions, and vibrant cultures. Standing only a few inches tall, Jaspey can sleep inside coat pockets, teacups, backpacks, hollow books, hat brims, or even small tins. Entire hidden settlements may exist unnoticed inside old walls, cemetery gardens, forgotten cupboards, abandoned wagons, or the roots of ancient trees. Jaspey are natural scavengers, herbalists, healers, thieves, potion brewers, and strange little mystics. They collect shiny objects obsessively, form strong emotional bonds with larger companions, and often travel alongside adventuring parties as medics, alchemists, scouts, or magical nuisances.

races.name: Elf
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Elf is selectable from the merged race dropdown/cache source. It uses the sheet package for Elf (Core Elves) unless your table overrides it. Elves are long-lived, graceful beings deeply attuned to magic, nature, and art. Their societies often value tradition, beauty, and refined mastery of specialized skills. They are broadly divided across many cultural and biological lineages throughout the worlds of the multiverse.
races.pantheonInfluence: Sigrananna shapes this lineage through forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.canonTraits: Darkvision, Elven Lineage, Fey Ancestry, Keen Senses, Trance (Player’s Handbook).
races.belavadosAbilities: Fey Memory: resistant to charm-like influence and skilled at trance, dream, or memory rituals.; Graceful Hunter: heightened perception and silent movement in wilderness or moonlit terrain.; Signature Expression: manifests a small but clear ancestral gift chosen with the DM to match the lineage.; Creator's Mark: Sigrananna leaves a cultural or supernatural bias toward forests, moonlight, wilderness, graceful hunters, and elven beauty.
races.dmHook: Use Elf (Core Elves) NPCs to show how Sigrananna's prison-era influence still appears in ancestry, culture, reputation, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Dril’thar
races.categoryDescription: Ancient mother of tides, reefs, hidden waters, and oceanic civilizations.
races.description: Dril’thar is selectable from the merged race dropdown/cache source. It uses the sheet package for Dril'thar unless your table overrides it. Amphibious Mystics of the Living Waters. Lore: The Dril'thar are an ancient amphibious people whose civilizations rise from swamps, flooded jungles, coral reefs, mangrove coasts, subterranean rivers, mist-choked marshes, and sacred rainforests. Across countless regions of the world, they are known as healers, navigators, mystics, hunters, survivalists, and wandering pilgrims capable of thriving where most civilizations would collapse. To outsiders, Dril'thar are often misunderstood as poisonous frogfolk, territorial swamp-creatures, or eerie marsh spirits. In truth, they are one of the most biologically adaptable and spiritually complex peoples in existence. Their philosophy teaches that all things move in cycles: rain becomes river, river becomes sea, decay becomes renewal, and death feeds life anew. Water is not merely an element to the Dril'thar—it is memory, ancestry, spirit, and destiny itself. A spawning pool may serve simultaneously as temple, cemetery, and place of prophecy, for the Dril'thar believe the currents of the world carry the echoes of those who came before.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Bastveig. Bastveig is the primary creator of the Dril'thar, binding amphibious mysticism, reef stewardship, hydromancy, and living water memory into their bodies. Dril'thar spawning pools are not only places of birth, but divine archives: their water is believed to remember every ancestor, every flood, every drowning, and every bargain made with the deep. Secondary Divine Pressures. Thoryn-Rahek appears in mutation myths, especially among lineages whose bodies adapt too quickly or in ways that feel alien even to other Dril'thar. Hathruna shapes marsh, rot, and renewal traditions where decay feeds pools and pools feed new life. Horundar influences ocean, storm, and flood clans who believe catastrophe can purify stagnant civilizations. Nefarokir is present in gravewater rites, where pools serve as cemeteries, memory wells, and thresholds for souls who died in water. Lineage Influence Notes. Grassland Dril'thar often honor Ishtanora for rain-fed survival and hidden wetland gardens. Swamp and marsh lineages commonly invoke Hathruna and Nefarokir together, treating rot and death as two halves of the same sacred current. Rainforest and forest lineages add Sigrananna to Bastveig's rites, while reef and ocean lineages are the most direct inheritors of Bastveig's deep-water civilization. Mutated or unusually colored children may be protected, feared, or consecrated depending on whether the clan reads the sign as Bastveig's blessing or Thoryn-Rahek's echo. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Pool of Names ceremony lets a clan recite its dead into living water so the next generation can dream them. The Tide Confession requires a Dril'thar to speak secrets into flowing water rather than into stone, because stillness traps guilt. Polluting a birth-pool, damming ancestral water without consent, or draining a cemetery-marsh are treated as sacrilege. Ichor-touched Dril'thar may develop black bioluminescent freckles, prophetic mucus patterns, voices that ripple water, or mutations that bloom after divine dreams.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase.; Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type.; You are a Humanoid.; Age.; Dril'thar hatch from gelatinous egg-clutches and rapidly mature during their early metamorphic stages. Most reach adulthood around age 10.; Typical Dril'thar live between 50 and 70 years, though certain deep-water or mystical bloodlines may survive for centuries.; Size.; Dril'thar vary dramatically between lineages and environmental adaptations.; Your size is either Small or Medium (choose when selecting this race).; Speed.; Your base walking speed is 30 feet. Best Classes: Ranger Monk
races.dmHook: Use Dril'thar NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Tamhiogals
races.categoryDescription: Ancient mother of tides, reefs, hidden waters, and oceanic civilizations.
races.description: Tamhiogals is selectable from the merged race dropdown/cache source. It uses the sheet package for Tamhiogal unless your table overrides it. Aquatic Bettafolk of Hydromancy, Coral Stewardship, and Tidal Spirits. Lore: Tamhiogal are aquatic beings. They resemble betta fish in their coloration, displaying vibrant shades of blues, purples, teals, and crimsons that shift with emotion and magical activity. On land, they possess scaled, graceful humanoid arms and legs, with scales rippling along their cheeks, necks, and collarbones. Their eyes are liquid and reflective, glowing faintly in dim light, and their hair consists of bioluminescent, jellyfish-like strands that can sting mildly when threatened. In water, they have gills along their necks, webbing between fingers and elbows, and fins along the spine, knees, and tail. Their legs are replaced by long, sleek caudal fins, with dorsal and anal fins enhancing maneuverability. Tamhiogal require hydration and tidal energy, which regenerate most efficiently in aquatic environments. They sustain themselves through fish, kelp, and other sea-based foods. Being immersed in water naturally maintains their hygiene and overall well-being. Exposure to polluted or stagnant water causes spiritual and emotional dissonance, while ritual bathing and communion with ocean spirits restore clarity, mood, and magical flow. Tamhiogal possess abilities attuned to both water and land. They can transition smoothly between terrestrial and aquatic forms, allowing fluid movement across both environments.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Bastveig. Bastveig is the primary creator of the Tamhiogal, binding bettafolk hydromancy, coral stewardship, tidal spirits, and aquatic civilization into their blood. Their vivid fins and emotional color-shifts are understood as reef-language inherited from the first living waters, where beauty, warning, courtship, grief, and magic were never separate things. Secondary Divine Pressures. Nephthysra is adored by Tamhiogal courts where beauty, charisma, emotional influence, and rare presence become unsettling divine inheritance. Horundar governs storms, floods, and violent tides, making him both feared and respected among open-ocean clans. Nefarokir is invoked in drowning rites and memorial currents, ensuring souls lost at sea are guided rather than trapped. Ishtanora appears in fishing villages and kelp farms where ocean harvest becomes family survival, while Valkhamesh is honored by royal fleets that claim dominion over harbors and sea-lanes. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Coral Naming lets a Tamhiogal place a fragment of coral, shell, or glass into a communal reef to mark a vow, birth, marriage, or grief. The Duel of Fins is a ritual conflict where beauty and restraint matter as much as victory. Poisoning a reef, enslaving a water spirit, or letting vanity endanger the community are major taboos. Ichor-touched Tamhiogal may develop black-rimmed fins, hypnotic scale patterns, water that curls toward their hands, or songs that make drowned memories surface. DM Hooks. Their pantheon influence supports coral courts, hydromancer diplomats, reef guardians, tempest exiles, beauty cults, drowned royal secrets, or Tamhiogal heroes trying to prove Nephthysra's charisma does not have to become manipulation.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Your Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Wisdom or Charisma score increases by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet. In aquatic form, you have a swimming speed of 40 feet.; Amphibious Tidal Form. You can breathe air and water. While fully immersed in water, your legs may shift into a sleek caudal fin, replacing your walking speed with your swimming speed until you return to land.; Hydromancy. You know shape water. At 3rd level, you may cast create or destroy water once per long rest. At 5th level, you may cast lesser restoration or warding bond once per long rest, flavored as healing currents or protective tides. Wisdom or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.; Bioluminescent Tendrils. Your jellyfish-like hair can glow with dim light in a 10-foot radius. When a creature grapples you or hits you with an unarmed strike, you may use your reaction to deal lightning or poison damage equal to your proficiency bonus.; Marine Communion. You can communicate simple ideas with aquatic beasts and spirits. You have advantage on Animal Handling, Nature, or Survival checks involving oceans, rivers, reefs, tides, kelp forests, or aquatic creatures.; Water-Sustained Wellbeing. You require regular hydration, ritual bathing, or immersion to remain healthy and magically balanced. Polluted or stagnant water can cause emotional and spiritual dissonance at the DM's discretion.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Aquan. Best Classes: Druid, Cleric, Sorcerer, Bard, Ranger, Paladin, or any oceanic guardian role.
races.dmHook: Use Tamhiogal NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Jaspeys
races.categoryDescription: Goddess of seasonal rebirth, decay, fungi, roots, and living wilderness.
races.description: Jaspeys is selectable from the merged race dropdown/cache source. It uses the sheet package for Jaspey unless your table overrides it. Ability Score Increase. Lore: Tiny fungal raccoon-folk born from ancient symbiotic spores and scavenger spirits, Jaspey are eerie yet lovable creatures that blur the line between woodland fae, living fungus, and grave-born scavenger. Though their appearance often resembles tiny undead raccoons stitched together with moss, mushrooms, and decay, Jaspey are fully living beings with beating hearts, warm emotions, and vibrant cultures. Standing only a few inches tall, Jaspey can sleep inside coat pockets, teacups, backpacks, hollow books, hat brims, or even small tins. Entire hidden settlements may exist unnoticed inside old walls, cemetery gardens, forgotten cupboards, abandoned wagons, or the roots of ancient trees. Jaspey are natural scavengers, herbalists, healers, thieves, potion brewers, and strange little mystics. They collect shiny objects obsessively, form strong emotional bonds with larger companions, and often travel alongside adventuring parties as medics, alchemists, scouts, or magical nuisances.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Hathruna. Hathruna is the primary creator of the Jaspey, blooming tiny fungal raccoon-folk from rot, spores, roots, scavenger spirits, and the cycle of decay into renewal. Jaspey theology teaches that nothing useful is truly trash: what the powerful discard becomes food, medicine, shelter, story, and sometimes rebellion. Secondary Divine Pressures. Ishtanora is a beloved hearth-patron to Jaspey villages because fungal gardens turn decay into comfort, medicine, and communal survival. Nefarokir is respected in grave-gardens where bones, memory, and compost are carefully separated so the dead are not stolen from their proper passage. Thoryn-Rahek is feared in uncontrolled spore blooms, violent mutations, and fungal hungers that no colony can name. Marduthor is honored by scavenger-crafters who turn broken locks, bottlecaps, wire, and discarded tools into practical magic. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The First Find is a rite where a young Jaspey brings home something abandoned and explains how it can serve the colony. The Soft Burial places beloved dead in protected fungal beds, not to erase them, but to let their last gifts become medicine or shelter. Wasting salvage, poisoning a communal spore-bed, or hoarding medicine during sickness are severe taboos. Ichor-touched Jaspey may grow black-edged mushrooms, dream through nearby fungus, or produce spores that briefly reveal hidden decay, lies, or sickness. DM Hooks. Use Jaspey pantheon influence for undercity colonies, fungal healers, sacred scavenger routes, rot-cults, grave-garden disputes, or tiny heroes who know exactly which forgotten scrap can save a doomed settlement.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase.; Your Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Wisdom or Intelligence score increases by 1.; Age.; Jaspey mature rapidly, reaching adulthood around 4 years old. Most live between 25 and 40 years, though ancient fungal sages and colony elders sometimes survive for over a century under ideal conditions.; Alignment.; Jaspey tend toward chaotic or neutral alignments. They value adaptability, freedom, comfort, curiosity, and survival more than laws or rigid traditions.; Most Jaspey are not malicious, though some become selfish scavengers, manipulative tricksters, grave robbers, or unsettling fungal occultists.; Creature Type.; You are a Humanoid.; You are also considered a Plant for any prerequisite, spell, magical effect, or item that specifically affects plant creatures.; Size.; You are Tiny.
races.dmHook: Use Jaspey NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Dragonborn — Noitzideq (Air and Weather)
races.categoryDescription: Creator of thunder-blooded sovereign races tied to ancient power and domination.
races.description: Dragonborn — Noitzideq (Air and Weather) is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Dragonborn. Theme: Air and Weather. Parentage / divine pressure: Asaafi + Sauqonsex, with Boisein storm influence. Inherited expression: Sky, weather, air/wind, storm movement, and healing currents. Dragonborn are draconic humanoids born of ancient dragon lineage, elemental force, dynastic memory, and divine sovereignty. In Belavadös, a Dragonborn may carry traditional chromatic, metallic, or gem ancestry while also belonging to a Belavadös dragon-parentage lineage.
races.pantheonInfluence: Valkhamesh and Nebyrr created the Dragonborn as scaled heirs of dragon parentage, sovereignty, storm-born dominion, lineage law, and dynastic memory. Creator God: Valkhamesh. Valkhamesh is honored as the creator of draconic bloodlines, sovereignty, storms, and ancient dominion. Dragonborn are not treated as a single uniform people; each lineage carries the mark of its dragon parentage, but the first divine ignition of draconic ancestry is traced to Valkhamesh's storm-bound authority. Scales, breath, wings, claws, and lineage instincts are read as living proof that ancient dominion still moves through mortal bodies. Secondary Divine Pressures. Nebyrr shapes Dragonborn inheritance, marriage law, dynasty records, oaths of bloodline legitimacy, and the legal status of mixed parentage. Enkirael influences war-clutches, conquest cults, honor-duels, and militant lineages that measure worth through victory. Horundar pulls oceanic, storm, volcanic, and pressure-bound lineages toward elemental extremes. Marduthor is revered by forge-scale clans that turn draconic anatomy into architecture, armor, and empire-building craft. Sigrananna influences forest, moonlit, green, and fey-adjacent dragonborn lineages that guard sacred groves or ecological pacts. Nefarokir governs funerary rites for dragonborn whose bloodlines remember the dead too strongly. Lineage Divine Use. A Dragonborn lineage can keep Valkhamesh as its creator-god while adding a patron pressure based on the parent dragons. Air, storm, and weather lineages often invoke Valkhamesh and Horundar together. Mountain, stone, crystal, and forge lineages often add Marduthor or Anubaldir. Ocean, tide, river, and reef lineages often add Bastveig or Horundar. Shadow, light, dream, and illusion lineages may add Raeshkul, Nephthysra, or Nefarokir depending on whether the culture frames the power as revelation, charisma, or liminal haunting. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Storm-Scale Naming is the most common birth rite: a hatchling or newly acknowledged Dragonborn is presented beneath open sky, near flame, stone, sea, or shadow according to lineage, and elders name the first dragon virtue seen in the child. The Bloodline Ledger is maintained by priests of Nebyrr so inheritance does not become mere conquest. A common taboo forbids claiming another Dragonborn's parentage without proof, because false bloodline claims are considered theft from Valkhamesh and fraud against Nebyrr. Ichor signs include storm-bright veins beneath the scales, breath that sparks before strong emotion, scale patterns resembling old territorial maps, and dreams of ancient wings over the world. Heritage focus: Air and Weather. Parentage: Asaafi + Sauqonsex, with Boisein storm influence.
races.canonTraits: Dragon Ancestry, Breath Weapon, Damage Resistance, Darkvision, Draconic Flight. Combined Ability Rule: A Belavadös Dragonborn gains the traditional Dragonborn trait package and the parentage features of its Belavadös dragon lineage. The breath, resistance, perception, and flight expressions remain active as standard draconic inheritance; the parentage features add bloodline-specific magic, senses, movement, survival, social, and elemental gifts. Belavadös Dragonborn Trait Framework: Ability Score Increase: Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type: You are a Humanoid with draconic ancestry.; Size: You are Medium.; Speed: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Darkvision: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.; Draconic Body: You have scaled skin, claws, a powerful tail, and facial ridges tied to your bloodline. Your claws are natural weapons that deal 1d6 slashing damage. You also have advantage on ability checks or saving throws made to keep your balance.; Winged Heritage: Your wings are part of your natural body. When you fall and are not incapacitated, you may use your reaction to reduce falling damage by an amount equal to five times your level. Starting at 5th level, you may fly for 1 minute at a flying speed equal to your walking speed. Once you use this flight, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.; Elemental Channeling: Choose one Dragonborn lineage. You gain its parentage ability package. When a lineage ability requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier, chosen when you select this race.; Ancestral Presence: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Arcana, History, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, or Religion.; Languages: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic. Belavadös Dragon-Parentage Lineages: Noitzideq (Air and Weather): parent dragons Asaafi + Sauqonsex, with Boisein storm influence; inherited theme Sky, weather, air/wind, storm movement, and healing currents.; profile Neutral Coordinator; scores Altruism 2000, Lawfulness 1650, Cooperation 2200, Honor 1850; Saelftum (Volcanic Flame): parent dragons Erymasos + Oreilhya, with Zrom elemental pressure; inherited theme Fire, heat, molten resilience, temperature control, and volcanic endurance.; profile Lawful Defender; scores Altruism 1650, Lawfulness 2050, Cooperation 1700, Honor 1750; Jazwed (Lava and Crystal): parent dragons Erymasos + Nenshexeq + Zrom; inherited theme Fire, stone, crystal force, elemental shaping, and defensive engineering.; profile Lawful Enforcer; scores Altruism 1600, Lawfulness 2100, Cooperation 1550, Honor 1700; Khoalaros (Tide and Ocean): parent dragons Philous + Xuqhuath + Yichah; inherited theme Sea control, water shaping, currents, swimming grace, aquatic communication, and healing water.; profile Neutral Guardian; scores Altruism 2050, Lawfulness 1500, Cooperation 2100, Honor 2000; Gauksneth (Desert Sand and Hidden Water): parent dragons Innoxia + Nenshexeq + Yichah/Xuqhuath; inherited theme Earth shaping, stone sense, sand survival, hidden-water detection, and oasis stewardship.; profile Lawful Coordinator; scores Altruism 1700, Lawfulness 2200, Cooperation 2050, Honor 1600; Tuorszom (Stone and Mountain): parent dragons Nenshexeq + Innoxia + Aloic; inherited theme Stone control, earth shaping, metal/mineral awareness, tremor sense, and mountain endurance.; profile Lawful Sentinel; scores Altruism 1800, Lawfulness 2300, Cooperation 1600, Honor 2250; Taudinax (Shadow and Light): parent dragons Ekhoq + Pteris + Xacuacsih/Thusekha, with Kiczi illusion influence; inherited theme Shadow manipulation, darkness, illusion, tactical concealment, and controlled light.; profile Neutral Strategist; scores Altruism 1450, Lawfulness 1300, Cooperation 1450, Honor 1350; Gouxelq (Metal and Crystal): parent dragons Aloic + Nenshexeq + Zrom; inherited theme Metal control, stone/crystal shaping, elemental structure, and crafted barriers.; profile Lawful Coordinator; scores Altruism 1750, Lawfulness 2300, Cooperation 2250, Honor 1700; Vautaces (Radiance and Foresight): parent dragons Thusekha + Xacuacsih + Mumh + Lunngha/Eecors + Chiellieds; inherited theme Light control, radiant protection, perception, foresight, time-sense, and emotional insight.; profile Lawful Arbiter; scores Altruism 1900, Lawfulness 2150, Cooperation 1550, Honor 2300. Heritage/Bloodline: Dragonborn — Noitzideq (Air and Weather). Sky, weather, air/wind, storm movement, and healing currents.
races.belavadosAbilities: Heritage/Bloodline: Dragonborn — Noitzideq (Air and Weather); Theme: Air and Weather; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Asaafi + Sauqonsex, with Boisein storm influence; Inherited Expression: Sky, weather, air/wind, storm movement, and healing currents
races.dmHook: Use Dragonborn NPCs for disputed heirs, storm-sovereign cults, dragon-parentage trials, bloodline fraud, conquest marriages, ancient dragon relics, forbidden lineage experiments, and descendants trying to turn dominion into protection.

races.name: Dragonborn — Saelftum (Volcanic Flame)
races.categoryDescription: Creator of thunder-blooded sovereign races tied to ancient power and domination.
races.description: Dragonborn — Saelftum (Volcanic Flame) is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Dragonborn. Theme: Volcanic Flame. Parentage / divine pressure: Erymasos + Oreilhya, with Zrom elemental pressure. Inherited expression: Fire, heat, molten resilience, temperature control, and volcanic endurance. Dragonborn are draconic humanoids born of ancient dragon lineage, elemental force, dynastic memory, and divine sovereignty. In Belavadös, a Dragonborn may carry traditional chromatic, metallic, or gem ancestry while also belonging to a Belavadös dragon-parentage lineage.
races.pantheonInfluence: Valkhamesh and Nebyrr created the Dragonborn as scaled heirs of dragon parentage, sovereignty, storm-born dominion, lineage law, and dynastic memory. Creator God: Valkhamesh. Valkhamesh is honored as the creator of draconic bloodlines, sovereignty, storms, and ancient dominion. Dragonborn are not treated as a single uniform people; each lineage carries the mark of its dragon parentage, but the first divine ignition of draconic ancestry is traced to Valkhamesh's storm-bound authority. Scales, breath, wings, claws, and lineage instincts are read as living proof that ancient dominion still moves through mortal bodies. Secondary Divine Pressures. Nebyrr shapes Dragonborn inheritance, marriage law, dynasty records, oaths of bloodline legitimacy, and the legal status of mixed parentage. Enkirael influences war-clutches, conquest cults, honor-duels, and militant lineages that measure worth through victory. Horundar pulls oceanic, storm, volcanic, and pressure-bound lineages toward elemental extremes. Marduthor is revered by forge-scale clans that turn draconic anatomy into architecture, armor, and empire-building craft. Sigrananna influences forest, moonlit, green, and fey-adjacent dragonborn lineages that guard sacred groves or ecological pacts. Nefarokir governs funerary rites for dragonborn whose bloodlines remember the dead too strongly. Lineage Divine Use. A Dragonborn lineage can keep Valkhamesh as its creator-god while adding a patron pressure based on the parent dragons. Air, storm, and weather lineages often invoke Valkhamesh and Horundar together. Mountain, stone, crystal, and forge lineages often add Marduthor or Anubaldir. Ocean, tide, river, and reef lineages often add Bastveig or Horundar. Shadow, light, dream, and illusion lineages may add Raeshkul, Nephthysra, or Nefarokir depending on whether the culture frames the power as revelation, charisma, or liminal haunting. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Storm-Scale Naming is the most common birth rite: a hatchling or newly acknowledged Dragonborn is presented beneath open sky, near flame, stone, sea, or shadow according to lineage, and elders name the first dragon virtue seen in the child. The Bloodline Ledger is maintained by priests of Nebyrr so inheritance does not become mere conquest. A common taboo forbids claiming another Dragonborn's parentage without proof, because false bloodline claims are considered theft from Valkhamesh and fraud against Nebyrr. Ichor signs include storm-bright veins beneath the scales, breath that sparks before strong emotion, scale patterns resembling old territorial maps, and dreams of ancient wings over the world. Heritage focus: Volcanic Flame. Parentage: Erymasos + Oreilhya, with Zrom elemental pressure.

races.name: Dragonborn — Gauksneth (Desert Sand and Hidden Water)
races.categoryDescription: Creator of thunder-blooded sovereign races tied to ancient power and domination.
races.description: Dragonborn — Gauksneth (Desert Sand and Hidden Water) is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Dragonborn. Theme: Desert Sand and Hidden Water. Parentage / divine pressure: Innoxia + Nenshexeq + Yichah/Xuqhuath. Inherited expression: Earth shaping, stone sense, sand survival, hidden-water detection, and oasis stewardship. Dragonborn are draconic humanoids born of ancient dragon lineage, elemental force, dynastic memory, and divine sovereignty. In Belavadös, a Dragonborn may carry traditional chromatic, metallic, or gem ancestry while also belonging to a Belavadös dragon-parentage lineage.
races.pantheonInfluence: Valkhamesh and Nebyrr created the Dragonborn as scaled heirs of dragon parentage, sovereignty, storm-born dominion, lineage law, and dynastic memory. Creator God: Valkhamesh. Valkhamesh is honored as the creator of draconic bloodlines, sovereignty, storms, and ancient dominion. Dragonborn are not treated as a single uniform people; each lineage carries the mark of its dragon parentage, but the first divine ignition of draconic ancestry is traced to Valkhamesh's storm-bound authority. Scales, breath, wings, claws, and lineage instincts are read as living proof that ancient dominion still moves through mortal bodies. Secondary Divine Pressures. Nebyrr shapes Dragonborn inheritance, marriage law, dynasty records, oaths of bloodline legitimacy, and the legal status of mixed parentage. Enkirael influences war-clutches, conquest cults, honor-duels, and militant lineages that measure worth through victory. Horundar pulls oceanic, storm, volcanic, and pressure-bound lineages toward elemental extremes. Marduthor is revered by forge-scale clans that turn draconic anatomy into architecture, armor, and empire-building craft. Sigrananna influences forest, moonlit, green, and fey-adjacent dragonborn lineages that guard sacred groves or ecological pacts. Nefarokir governs funerary rites for dragonborn whose bloodlines remember the dead too strongly. Lineage Divine Use. A Dragonborn lineage can keep Valkhamesh as its creator-god while adding a patron pressure based on the parent dragons. Air, storm, and weather lineages often invoke Valkhamesh and Horundar together. Mountain, stone, crystal, and forge lineages often add Marduthor or Anubaldir. Ocean, tide, river, and reef lineages often add Bastveig or Horundar. Shadow, light, dream, and illusion lineages may add Raeshkul, Nephthysra, or Nefarokir depending on whether the culture frames the power as revelation, charisma, or liminal haunting. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Storm-Scale Naming is the most common birth rite: a hatchling or newly acknowledged Dragonborn is presented beneath open sky, near flame, stone, sea, or shadow according to lineage, and elders name the first dragon virtue seen in the child. The Bloodline Ledger is maintained by priests of Nebyrr so inheritance does not become mere conquest. A common taboo forbids claiming another Dragonborn's parentage without proof, because false bloodline claims are considered theft from Valkhamesh and fraud against Nebyrr. Ichor signs include storm-bright veins beneath the scales, breath that sparks before strong emotion, scale patterns resembling old territorial maps, and dreams of ancient wings over the world. Heritage focus: Desert Sand and Hidden Water. Parentage: Innoxia + Nenshexeq + Yichah/Xuqhuath.
races.canonTraits: Dragon Ancestry, Breath Weapon, Damage Resistance, Darkvision, Draconic Flight. Combined Ability Rule: A Belavadös Dragonborn gains the traditional Dragonborn trait package and the parentage features of its Belavadös dragon lineage. The breath, resistance, perception, and flight expressions remain active as standard draconic inheritance; the parentage features add bloodline-specific magic, senses, movement, survival, social, and elemental gifts. Belavadös Dragonborn Trait Framework: Ability Score Increase: Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type: You are a Humanoid with draconic ancestry.; Size: You are Medium.; Speed: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Darkvision: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.; Draconic Body: You have scaled skin, claws, a powerful tail, and facial ridges tied to your bloodline. Your claws are natural weapons that deal 1d6 slashing damage. You also have advantage on ability checks or saving throws made to keep your balance.; Winged Heritage: Your wings are part of your natural body. When you fall and are not incapacitated, you may use your reaction to reduce falling damage by an amount equal to five times your level. Starting at 5th level, you may fly for 1 minute at a flying speed equal to your walking speed. Once you use this flight, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.; Elemental Channeling: Choose one Dragonborn lineage. You gain its parentage ability package. When a lineage ability requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier, chosen when you select this race.; Ancestral Presence: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Arcana, History, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, or Religion.; Languages: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic. Belavadös Dragon-Parentage Lineages: Noitzideq (Air and Weather): parent dragons Asaafi + Sauqonsex, with Boisein storm influence; inherited theme Sky, weather, air/wind, storm movement, and healing currents.; profile Neutral Coordinator; scores Altruism 2000, Lawfulness 1650, Cooperation 2200, Honor 1850; Saelftum (Volcanic Flame): parent dragons Erymasos + Oreilhya, with Zrom elemental pressure; inherited theme Fire, heat, molten resilience, temperature control, and volcanic endurance.; profile Lawful Defender; scores Altruism 1650, Lawfulness 2050, Cooperation 1700, Honor 1750; Jazwed (Lava and Crystal): parent dragons Erymasos + Nenshexeq + Zrom; inherited theme Fire, stone, crystal force, elemental shaping, and defensive engineering.; profile Lawful Enforcer; scores Altruism 1600, Lawfulness 2100, Cooperation 1550, Honor 1700; Khoalaros (Tide and Ocean): parent dragons Philous + Xuqhuath + Yichah; inherited theme Sea control, water shaping, currents, swimming grace, aquatic communication, and healing water.; profile Neutral Guardian; scores Altruism 2050, Lawfulness 1500, Cooperation 2100, Honor 2000; Gauksneth (Desert Sand and Hidden Water): parent dragons Innoxia + Nenshexeq + Yichah/Xuqhuath; inherited theme Earth shaping, stone sense, sand survival, hidden-water detection, and oasis stewardship.; profile Lawful Coordinator; scores Altruism 1700, Lawfulness 2200, Cooperation 2050, Honor 1600; Tuorszom (Stone and Mountain): parent dragons Nenshexeq + Innoxia + Aloic; inherited theme Stone control, earth shaping, metal/mineral awareness, tremor sense, and mountain endurance.; profile Lawful Sentinel; scores Altruism 1800, Lawfulness 2300, Cooperation 1600, Honor 2250; Taudinax (Shadow and Light): parent dragons Ekhoq + Pteris + Xacuacsih/Thusekha, with Kiczi illusion influence; inherited theme Shadow manipulation, darkness, illusion, tactical concealment, and controlled light.; profile Neutral Strategist; scores Altruism 1450, Lawfulness 1300, Cooperation 1450, Honor 1350; Gouxelq (Metal and Crystal): parent dragons Aloic + Nenshexeq + Zrom; inherited theme Metal control, stone/crystal shaping, elemental structure, and crafted barriers.; profile Lawful Coordinator; scores Altruism 1750, Lawfulness 2300, Cooperation 2250, Honor 1700; Vautaces (Radiance and Foresight): parent dragons Thusekha + Xacuacsih + Mumh + Lunngha/Eecors + Chiellieds; inherited theme Light control, radiant protection, perception, foresight, time-sense, and emotional insight.; profile Lawful Arbiter; scores Altruism 1900, Lawfulness 2150, Cooperation 1550, Honor 2300. Heritage/Bloodline: Dragonborn — Gauksneth (Desert Sand and Hidden Water). Earth shaping, stone sense, sand survival, hidden-water detection, and oasis stewardship.

races.name: Dragonborn — Gouxelq (Metal and Crystal)
races.categoryDescription: Creator of thunder-blooded sovereign races tied to ancient power and domination.
races.description: Dragonborn — Gouxelq (Metal and Crystal) is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Dragonborn. Theme: Metal and Crystal. Parentage / divine pressure: Aloic + Nenshexeq + Zrom. Inherited expression: Metal control, stone/crystal shaping, elemental structure, and crafted barriers. Dragonborn are draconic humanoids born of ancient dragon lineage, elemental force, dynastic memory, and divine sovereignty. In Belavadös, a Dragonborn may carry traditional chromatic, metallic, or gem ancestry while also belonging to a Belavadös dragon-parentage lineage.
races.pantheonInfluence: Valkhamesh and Nebyrr created the Dragonborn as scaled heirs of dragon parentage, sovereignty, storm-born dominion, lineage law, and dynastic memory. Creator God: Valkhamesh. Valkhamesh is honored as the creator of draconic bloodlines, sovereignty, storms, and ancient dominion. Dragonborn are not treated as a single uniform people; each lineage carries the mark of its dragon parentage, but the first divine ignition of draconic ancestry is traced to Valkhamesh's storm-bound authority. Scales, breath, wings, claws, and lineage instincts are read as living proof that ancient dominion still moves through mortal bodies. Secondary Divine Pressures. Nebyrr shapes Dragonborn inheritance, marriage law, dynasty records, oaths of bloodline legitimacy, and the legal status of mixed parentage. Enkirael influences war-clutches, conquest cults, honor-duels, and militant lineages that measure worth through victory. Horundar pulls oceanic, storm, volcanic, and pressure-bound lineages toward elemental extremes. Marduthor is revered by forge-scale clans that turn draconic anatomy into architecture, armor, and empire-building craft. Sigrananna influences forest, moonlit, green, and fey-adjacent dragonborn lineages that guard sacred groves or ecological pacts. Nefarokir governs funerary rites for dragonborn whose bloodlines remember the dead too strongly. Lineage Divine Use. A Dragonborn lineage can keep Valkhamesh as its creator-god while adding a patron pressure based on the parent dragons. Air, storm, and weather lineages often invoke Valkhamesh and Horundar together. Mountain, stone, crystal, and forge lineages often add Marduthor or Anubaldir. Ocean, tide, river, and reef lineages often add Bastveig or Horundar. Shadow, light, dream, and illusion lineages may add Raeshkul, Nephthysra, or Nefarokir depending on whether the culture frames the power as revelation, charisma, or liminal haunting. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Storm-Scale Naming is the most common birth rite: a hatchling or newly acknowledged Dragonborn is presented beneath open sky, near flame, stone, sea, or shadow according to lineage, and elders name the first dragon virtue seen in the child. The Bloodline Ledger is maintained by priests of Nebyrr so inheritance does not become mere conquest. A common taboo forbids claiming another Dragonborn's parentage without proof, because false bloodline claims are considered theft from Valkhamesh and fraud against Nebyrr. Ichor signs include storm-bright veins beneath the scales, breath that sparks before strong emotion, scale patterns resembling old territorial maps, and dreams of ancient wings over the world. Heritage focus: Metal and Crystal. Parentage: Aloic + Nenshexeq + Zrom.
races.canonTraits: Dragon Ancestry, Breath Weapon, Damage Resistance, Darkvision, Draconic Flight. Combined Ability Rule: A Belavadös Dragonborn gains the traditional Dragonborn trait package and the parentage features of its Belavadös dragon lineage. The breath, resistance, perception, and flight expressions remain active as standard draconic inheritance; the parentage features add bloodline-specific magic, senses, movement, survival, social, and elemental gifts. Belavadös Dragonborn Trait Framework: Ability Score Increase: Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type: You are a Humanoid with draconic ancestry.; Size: You are Medium.; Speed: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Darkvision: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.; Draconic Body: You have scaled skin, claws, a powerful tail, and facial ridges tied to your bloodline. Your claws are natural weapons that deal 1d6 slashing damage. You also have advantage on ability checks or saving throws made to keep your balance.; Winged Heritage: Your wings are part of your natural body. When you fall and are not incapacitated, you may use your reaction to reduce falling damage by an amount equal to five times your level. Starting at 5th level, you may fly for 1 minute at a flying speed equal to your walking speed. Once you use this flight, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.; Elemental Channeling: Choose one Dragonborn lineage. You gain its parentage ability package. When a lineage ability requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier, chosen when you select this race.; Ancestral Presence: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Arcana, History, Intimidation, Nature, Perception, or Religion.; Languages: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic. Belavadös Dragon-Parentage Lineages: Noitzideq (Air and Weather): parent dragons Asaafi + Sauqonsex, with Boisein storm influence; inherited theme Sky, weather, air/wind, storm movement, and healing currents.; profile Neutral Coordinator; scores Altruism 2000, Lawfulness 1650, Cooperation 2200, Honor 1850; Saelftum (Volcanic Flame): parent dragons Erymasos + Oreilhya, with Zrom elemental pressure; inherited theme Fire, heat, molten resilience, temperature control, and volcanic endurance.; profile Lawful Defender; scores Altruism 1650, Lawfulness 2050, Cooperation 1700, Honor 1750; Jazwed (Lava and Crystal): parent dragons Erymasos + Nenshexeq + Zrom; inherited theme Fire, stone, crystal force, elemental shaping, and defensive engineering.; profile Lawful Enforcer; scores Altruism 1600, Lawfulness 2100, Cooperation 1550, Honor 1700; Khoalaros (Tide and Ocean): parent dragons Philous + Xuqhuath + Yichah; inherited theme Sea control, water shaping, currents, swimming grace, aquatic communication, and healing water.; profile Neutral Guardian; scores Altruism 2050, Lawfulness 1500, Cooperation 2100, Honor 2000; Gauksneth (Desert Sand and Hidden Water): parent dragons Innoxia + Nenshexeq + Yichah/Xuqhuath; inherited theme Earth shaping, stone sense, sand survival, hidden-water detection, and oasis stewardship.; profile Lawful Coordinator; scores Altruism 1700, Lawfulness 2200, Cooperation 2050, Honor 1600; Tuorszom (Stone and Mountain): parent dragons Nenshexeq + Innoxia + Aloic; inherited theme Stone control, earth shaping, metal/mineral awareness, tremor sense, and mountain endurance.; profile Lawful Sentinel; scores Altruism 1800, Lawfulness 2300, Cooperation 1600, Honor 2250; Taudinax (Shadow and Light): parent dragons Ekhoq + Pteris + Xacuacsih/Thusekha, with Kiczi illusion influence; inherited theme Shadow manipulation, darkness, illusion, tactical concealment, and controlled light.; profile Neutral Strategist; scores Altruism 1450, Lawfulness 1300, Cooperation 1450, Honor 1350; Gouxelq (Metal and Crystal): parent dragons Aloic + Nenshexeq + Zrom; inherited theme Metal control, stone/crystal shaping, elemental structure, and crafted barriers.; profile Lawful Coordinator; scores Altruism 1750, Lawfulness 2300, Cooperation 2250, Honor 1700; Vautaces (Radiance and Foresight): parent dragons Thusekha + Xacuacsih + Mumh + Lunngha/Eecors + Chiellieds; inherited theme Light control, radiant protection, perception, foresight, time-sense, and emotional insight.; profile Lawful Arbiter; scores Altruism 1900, Lawfulness 2150, Cooperation 1550, Honor 2300. Heritage/Bloodline: Dragonborn — Gouxelq (Metal and Crystal). Metal control, stone/crystal shaping, elemental structure, and crafted barriers.

races.name: Dril'thar — Grassland Lineage
races.categoryDescription: Ancient mother of tides, reefs, hidden waters, and oceanic civilizations.
races.description: Dril'thar — Grassland Lineage is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Dril'thar. Theme: Rain-fed grassland wetlands, hidden wetland gardens, and prairie survival. Parentage / divine pressure: Ishtanora rain-fed survival influence with Bastveig water memory. Inherited expression: Rain-fed grassland wetlands, hidden wetland gardens, and prairie survival. Amphibious Mystics of the Living Waters. Lore: The Dril'thar are an ancient amphibious people whose civilizations rise from swamps, flooded jungles, coral reefs, mangrove coasts, subterranean rivers, mist-choked marshes, and sacred rainforests. Across countless regions of the world, they are known as healers, navigators, mystics, hunters, survivalists, and wandering pilgrims capable of thriving where most civilizations would collapse. To outsiders, Dril'thar are often misunderstood as poisonous frogfolk, territorial swamp-creatures, or eerie marsh spirits. In truth, they are one of the most biologically adaptable and spiritually complex peoples in existence. Their philosophy teaches that all things move in cycles: rain becomes river, river becomes sea, decay becomes renewal, and death feeds life anew. Water is not merely an element to the Dril'thar—it is memory, ancestry, spirit, and destiny itself. A spawning pool may serve simultaneously as temple, cemetery, and place of prophecy, for the Dril'thar believe the currents of the world carry the echoes of those who came before.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Bastveig. Bastveig is the primary creator of the Dril'thar, binding amphibious mysticism, reef stewardship, hydromancy, and living water memory into their bodies. Dril'thar spawning pools are not only places of birth, but divine archives: their water is believed to remember every ancestor, every flood, every drowning, and every bargain made with the deep. Secondary Divine Pressures. Thoryn-Rahek appears in mutation myths, especially among lineages whose bodies adapt too quickly or in ways that feel alien even to other Dril'thar. Hathruna shapes marsh, rot, and renewal traditions where decay feeds pools and pools feed new life. Horundar influences ocean, storm, and flood clans who believe catastrophe can purify stagnant civilizations. Nefarokir is present in gravewater rites, where pools serve as cemeteries, memory wells, and thresholds for souls who died in water. Lineage Influence Notes. Grassland Dril'thar often honor Ishtanora for rain-fed survival and hidden wetland gardens. Swamp and marsh lineages commonly invoke Hathruna and Nefarokir together, treating rot and death as two halves of the same sacred current. Rainforest and forest lineages add Sigrananna to Bastveig's rites, while reef and ocean lineages are the most direct inheritors of Bastveig's deep-water civilization. Mutated or unusually colored children may be protected, feared, or consecrated depending on whether the clan reads the sign as Bastveig's blessing or Thoryn-Rahek's echo. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Pool of Names ceremony lets a clan recite its dead into living water so the next generation can dream them. The Tide Confession requires a Dril'thar to speak secrets into flowing water rather than into stone, because stillness traps guilt. Polluting a birth-pool, damming ancestral water without consent, or draining a cemetery-marsh are treated as sacrilege. Ichor-touched Dril'thar may develop black bioluminescent freckles, prophetic mucus patterns, voices that ripple water, or mutations that bloom after divine dreams. Heritage focus: Rain-fed grassland wetlands, hidden wetland gardens, and prairie survival. Parentage: Ishtanora rain-fed survival influence with Bastveig water memory.
races.canonTraits: Heritage/Bloodline: Dril'thar — Grassland Lineage. Rain-fed grassland wetlands, hidden wetland gardens, and prairie survival.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase.; Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type.; You are a Humanoid.; Age.; Dril'thar hatch from gelatinous egg-clutches and rapidly mature during their early metamorphic stages. Most reach adulthood around age 10.; Typical Dril'thar live between 50 and 70 years, though certain deep-water or mystical bloodlines may survive for centuries.; Size.; Dril'thar vary dramatically between lineages and environmental adaptations.; Your size is either Small or Medium (choose when selecting this race).; Speed.; Your base walking speed is 30 feet. Best Classes: Ranger Monk; Heritage/Bloodline: Dril'thar — Grassland Lineage; Theme: Rain-fed grassland wetlands, hidden wetland gardens, and prairie survival; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Ishtanora rain-fed survival influence with Bastveig water memory; Inherited Expression: Rain-fed grassland wetlands, hidden wetland gardens, and prairie survival
races.dmHook: Use Dril'thar NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Dril'thar — Marsh Lineage
races.categoryDescription: Ancient mother of tides, reefs, hidden waters, and oceanic civilizations.
races.description: Dril'thar — Marsh Lineage is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Dril'thar. Theme: Mist, reeds, ambush waterways, cemetery marshes, and liminal waters. Parentage / divine pressure: Hathruna renewal and Nefarokir threshold rites. Inherited expression: Mist, reeds, ambush waterways, cemetery marshes, and liminal waters. Amphibious Mystics of the Living Waters. Lore: The Dril'thar are an ancient amphibious people whose civilizations rise from swamps, flooded jungles, coral reefs, mangrove coasts, subterranean rivers, mist-choked marshes, and sacred rainforests. Across countless regions of the world, they are known as healers, navigators, mystics, hunters, survivalists, and wandering pilgrims capable of thriving where most civilizations would collapse. To outsiders, Dril'thar are often misunderstood as poisonous frogfolk, territorial swamp-creatures, or eerie marsh spirits. In truth, they are one of the most biologically adaptable and spiritually complex peoples in existence. Their philosophy teaches that all things move in cycles: rain becomes river, river becomes sea, decay becomes renewal, and death feeds life anew. Water is not merely an element to the Dril'thar—it is memory, ancestry, spirit, and destiny itself. A spawning pool may serve simultaneously as temple, cemetery, and place of prophecy, for the Dril'thar believe the currents of the world carry the echoes of those who came before.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Bastveig. Bastveig is the primary creator of the Dril'thar, binding amphibious mysticism, reef stewardship, hydromancy, and living water memory into their bodies. Dril'thar spawning pools are not only places of birth, but divine archives: their water is believed to remember every ancestor, every flood, every drowning, and every bargain made with the deep. Secondary Divine Pressures. Thoryn-Rahek appears in mutation myths, especially among lineages whose bodies adapt too quickly or in ways that feel alien even to other Dril'thar. Hathruna shapes marsh, rot, and renewal traditions where decay feeds pools and pools feed new life. Horundar influences ocean, storm, and flood clans who believe catastrophe can purify stagnant civilizations. Nefarokir is present in gravewater rites, where pools serve as cemeteries, memory wells, and thresholds for souls who died in water. Lineage Influence Notes. Grassland Dril'thar often honor Ishtanora for rain-fed survival and hidden wetland gardens. Swamp and marsh lineages commonly invoke Hathruna and Nefarokir together, treating rot and death as two halves of the same sacred current. Rainforest and forest lineages add Sigrananna to Bastveig's rites, while reef and ocean lineages are the most direct inheritors of Bastveig's deep-water civilization. Mutated or unusually colored children may be protected, feared, or consecrated depending on whether the clan reads the sign as Bastveig's blessing or Thoryn-Rahek's echo. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Pool of Names ceremony lets a clan recite its dead into living water so the next generation can dream them. The Tide Confession requires a Dril'thar to speak secrets into flowing water rather than into stone, because stillness traps guilt. Polluting a birth-pool, damming ancestral water without consent, or draining a cemetery-marsh are treated as sacrilege. Ichor-touched Dril'thar may develop black bioluminescent freckles, prophetic mucus patterns, voices that ripple water, or mutations that bloom after divine dreams. Heritage focus: Mist, reeds, ambush waterways, cemetery marshes, and liminal waters. Parentage: Hathruna renewal and Nefarokir threshold rites.
