races.name: Kaluseban — Arcane Bloodline
races.categoryDescription: Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
races.description: Kaluseban — Arcane Bloodline is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Kaluseban. Theme: Ley-line grace, scholar courts, arcane memory. Parentage / divine pressure: Sigrananna with Raeshkul-style study pressure. Inherited expression: Ley-line grace, scholar courts, arcane memory. Elegant Mana-Bound Humanoids of Ley Lines, Nature, Moonlight, and Shadow. Lore: Kalusebans are an elegant and enigmatic race of magical humanoids . They are tall and slender, with graceful features and skin tones that reflect their bloodline, ranging from pale ivory to deep sapphire or earthy greens and browns. Their eyes often glow faintly, carrying hues tied to their magical affinity, and their hair can shimmer, float slightly, or reflect celestial or natural motifs. Kalusebans carry an aura of otherworldly presence, appearing almost ethereal as if the ambient magic bends subtly around them. They thrive in environments rich with mana, nature, or celestial energy, and their forms are highly sensitive to ritual, lunar phases, and environmental harmony. Kalusebans require periodic exposure to magical energy, whether through ley lines, enchanted sites, or ceremonial practices, to maintain their vitality and arcane strength. They bond strongly with natural environments, finding balance and clarity among forests, rivers, or sacred groves. Rituals, music, meditation, or alignment with celestial bodies enhance their mental and emotional equilibrium. Deprivation from magic, polluted areas, or urban confines can weaken their vitality, induce restlessness, or diminish their magical attunement. They also maintain basic needs like nutrition, hydration, and hygiene, often observing these through symbolic or ceremonial acts that reinforce cultural traditions. Kalusebans have abilities tied to their bloodline and affinity. They can manipulate natural or arcane energies, commune with flora and fauna, sense ley lines or currents of magical energy, and subtly influence the environment around them.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Sigrananna. Sigrananna is the primary creator of the Kaluseban, shaping their ley-line grace, moonlit nature, and shadow-touched elegance from wilderness beauty and lunar power. Kaluseban bodies are often described as living ley-script: skin, eyes, hair, and bloodline traits reveal how moonlight, forest memory, and ambient magic wrote themselves into mortal form. Secondary Divine Pressures. Eirzunet haunts umbral bloodlines, forbidden transformations, cursecraft, and twilight bargains. Bastveig influences Tideborn Kaluseban who listen to reef, river, mist, and rain as extensions of living water memory. Horundar shapes Stonebound and storm-touched lines through pressure, earthquake, weather, and elemental endurance. Raeshkul is honored by arcane scholars who map ley currents as thought-patterns, while Thalunesh appears in dream rites and wandering moonlit pilgrimages. Bloodline Influence Notes. Arcane Kaluseban tend to frame Raeshkul and Sigrananna as paired forces: pattern and beauty. Starborne lines often add Sokhivar's prophecy and distant light. Wildroot lines belong most clearly to Sigrananna's forests, while Tideborn lines blend Sigrananna with Bastveig. Stonebound lines may honor Horundar or Anubaldir depending on whether they read stone as force or burden. Umbral Kaluseban are the most likely to carry Eirzunet's dangerous inheritance. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Moonroot Vigil is a night-long meditation at a ley crossing, where a Kaluseban listens for which god's hunger is nearest to their bloodline. Polluting a ley line, binding a grove without consent, or using beauty to hide predation are serious taboos. Ichor-touched Kaluseban may cast two shadows, glow under moonless skies, hear roots speaking in old languages, or develop silver-black veins that flare during eclipses. Heritage focus: Ley-line grace, scholar courts, arcane memory. Parentage: Sigrananna with Raeshkul-style study pressure.
races.canonTraits: Heritage/Bloodline: Kaluseban — Arcane Bloodline. Ley-line grace, scholar courts, arcane memory.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Mana-Bound Vitality. You require periodic exposure to magical energy, sacred natural sites, ley lines, celestial light, ritual practice, or enchanted environments to remain fully balanced. Long-term deprivation from magic may cause exhaustion, restlessness, or diminished spellcasting at the DM's discretion.; Leyline Sense. You gain proficiency in Arcana or Nature. You can sense whether a place has strong natural, celestial, arcane, shadow, aquatic, or earthen magical resonance after spending 1 minute in quiet focus.; Subtle Channeling. You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid, cleric, or wizard spell list. Wisdom, Intelligence, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it, chosen when you select this race.; Ritual Poise. You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed by illusions, dream effects, or magic that distorts your perception of reality.; Bloodline Affinity. Choose one Kaluseban bloodline. You gain the bloodline gift listed for it.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language tied to your home culture, bloodline, or magical tradition. Best Classes: Druid, Wizard, Cleric, Warlock, Bard, Ranger, Sorcerer, or any ritual-focused class.; Heritage/Bloodline: Kaluseban — Arcane Bloodline; Theme: Ley-line grace, scholar courts, arcane memory; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Sigrananna with Raeshkul-style study pressure; Inherited Expression: Ley-line grace, scholar courts, arcane memory
races.dmHook: Use Kaluseban NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.description: Kaluseban — Stonebound Bloodline is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Kaluseban. Theme: Moonlit stone, mountain vows, and endurance. Parentage / divine pressure: Sigrananna grace with Anubaldir/Marduthor endurance pressure. Inherited expression: Moonlit stone, mountain vows, and endurance. Elegant Mana-Bound Humanoids of Ley Lines, Nature, Moonlight, and Shadow. Lore: Kalusebans are an elegant and enigmatic race of magical humanoids . They are tall and slender, with graceful features and skin tones that reflect their bloodline, ranging from pale ivory to deep sapphire or earthy greens and browns. Their eyes often glow faintly, carrying hues tied to their magical affinity, and their hair can shimmer, float slightly, or reflect celestial or natural motifs. Kalusebans carry an aura of otherworldly presence, appearing almost ethereal as if the ambient magic bends subtly around them. They thrive in environments rich with mana, nature, or celestial energy, and their forms are highly sensitive to ritual, lunar phases, and environmental harmony. Kalusebans require periodic exposure to magical energy, whether through ley lines, enchanted sites, or ceremonial practices, to maintain their vitality and arcane strength. They bond strongly with natural environments, finding balance and clarity among forests, rivers, or sacred groves. Rituals, music, meditation, or alignment with celestial bodies enhance their mental and emotional equilibrium. Deprivation from magic, polluted areas, or urban confines can weaken their vitality, induce restlessness, or diminish their magical attunement. They also maintain basic needs like nutrition, hydration, and hygiene, often observing these through symbolic or ceremonial acts that reinforce cultural traditions. Kalusebans have abilities tied to their bloodline and affinity. They can manipulate natural or arcane energies, commune with flora and fauna, sense ley lines or currents of magical energy, and subtly influence the environment around them.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Sigrananna. Sigrananna is the primary creator of the Kaluseban, shaping their ley-line grace, moonlit nature, and shadow-touched elegance from wilderness beauty and lunar power. Kaluseban bodies are often described as living ley-script: skin, eyes, hair, and bloodline traits reveal how moonlight, forest memory, and ambient magic wrote themselves into mortal form. Secondary Divine Pressures. Eirzunet haunts umbral bloodlines, forbidden transformations, cursecraft, and twilight bargains. Bastveig influences Tideborn Kaluseban who listen to reef, river, mist, and rain as extensions of living water memory. Horundar shapes Stonebound and storm-touched lines through pressure, earthquake, weather, and elemental endurance. Raeshkul is honored by arcane scholars who map ley currents as thought-patterns, while Thalunesh appears in dream rites and wandering moonlit pilgrimages. Bloodline Influence Notes. Arcane Kaluseban tend to frame Raeshkul and Sigrananna as paired forces: pattern and beauty. Starborne lines often add Sokhivar's prophecy and distant light. Wildroot lines belong most clearly to Sigrananna's forests, while Tideborn lines blend Sigrananna with Bastveig. Stonebound lines may honor Horundar or Anubaldir depending on whether they read stone as force or burden. Umbral Kaluseban are the most likely to carry Eirzunet's dangerous inheritance. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Moonroot Vigil is a night-long meditation at a ley crossing, where a Kaluseban listens for which god's hunger is nearest to their bloodline. Polluting a ley line, binding a grove without consent, or using beauty to hide predation are serious taboos. Ichor-touched Kaluseban may cast two shadows, glow under moonless skies, hear roots speaking in old languages, or develop silver-black veins that flare during eclipses. Heritage focus: Moonlit stone, mountain vows, and endurance. Parentage: Sigrananna grace with Anubaldir/Marduthor endurance pressure.
races.canonTraits: Heritage/Bloodline: Kaluseban — Stonebound Bloodline. Moonlit stone, mountain vows, and endurance.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Mana-Bound Vitality. You require periodic exposure to magical energy, sacred natural sites, ley lines, celestial light, ritual practice, or enchanted environments to remain fully balanced. Long-term deprivation from magic may cause exhaustion, restlessness, or diminished spellcasting at the DM's discretion.; Leyline Sense. You gain proficiency in Arcana or Nature. You can sense whether a place has strong natural, celestial, arcane, shadow, aquatic, or earthen magical resonance after spending 1 minute in quiet focus.; Subtle Channeling. You know one cantrip of your choice from the druid, cleric, or wizard spell list. Wisdom, Intelligence, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it, chosen when you select this race.; Ritual Poise. You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed by illusions, dream effects, or magic that distorts your perception of reality.; Bloodline Affinity. Choose one Kaluseban bloodline. You gain the bloodline gift listed for it.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language tied to your home culture, bloodline, or magical tradition. Best Classes: Druid, Wizard, Cleric, Warlock, Bard, Ranger, Sorcerer, or any ritual-focused class.; Heritage/Bloodline: Kaluseban — Stonebound Bloodline; Theme: Moonlit stone, mountain vows, and endurance; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Sigrananna grace with Anubaldir/Marduthor endurance pressure; Inherited Expression: Moonlit stone, mountain vows, and endurance
races.dmHook: Use Kaluseban NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.name: Panolima — Earth Thread
races.categoryDescription: Master of storms, seas, earthquakes, floods, and untamed elemental force.
races.description: Panolima — Earth Thread is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Panolima. Theme: Stone, soil, pressure, endurance, and grounded elemental force. Parentage / divine pressure: Horundar earth pressure. Inherited expression: Stone, soil, pressure, endurance, and grounded elemental force. Living Embodiments of Elemental Harmony: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, and Aether. Lore: The Panolimas are beings of elemental harmony . Their bodies are infused with one of the five primordial elemental threads: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, or Aether. Each individual exhibits physical traits reflecting their elemental affinity, from stone-like skin and mossy hair to flowing, water-like forms or luminous, energy-filled auras. They thrive in environments aligned with their element, drawing sustenance, vitality, and energy from landscapes, currents, storms, magma, or ley lines. Their movements, gestures, and even voices often carry the rhythm of their element, and they engage in ritual cleansing or meditation to maintain harmony with their elemental thread. Earth Panolimas have skin patterned like bark or stone, hair resembling moss or soil, and a deep-toned, rumbling voice. They require contact with solid terrain, forests, caves, or mountains to maintain vitality. Their abilities include sensing structural weaknesses, reinforcing the land, and enduring physical hardships with exceptional resilience. They can read terrain, detect mineral-rich soils, and manipulate stone or plant life to shape their surroundings. Fire Panolimas exhibit cracks like magma across their skin, ember-red hair, and glowing eyes. They rely on heat sources, volcanic activity, and exposure to flames to sustain energy. Their bodies radiate warmth and light, and they can manipulate molten materials, summon bursts of flame, and purify areas through fire.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Horundar. Horundar is the primary creator of the Panolima, whose elemental threads of earth, fire, water, air, and aether embody the unstable boundaries of ocean, storm, pressure, and upheaval. Panolima do not merely use elements; they are living negotiations between forces that would otherwise break the world apart. Secondary Divine Pressures. Oskar'enlil studies Panolima balance as a living experiment in civilization, instinct, elemental pressure, and structured survival. Bastveig influences Water Panolima who treat currents and reefs as living memory. Marduthor appears among Fire and Earth Panolima who turn heat, stone, and metal into craft. Sigrananna shapes plant-rich Earth and Air communities through wilderness harmony, while Valkhamesh influences storm-born Panolima who see hierarchy in lightning and pressure. Elemental Influence Notes. Earth Panolima often honor Anubaldir, Marduthor, or Sigrananna depending on whether their culture emphasizes burden, craft, or roots. Fire Panolima may honor Marduthor's forge or Enkirael's battle-flame, creating a major ethical divide. Water Panolima most often answer Horundar and Bastveig together. Air Panolima add Sokhivar for song, prophecy, and sky freedom. Aether Panolima are frequently watched by Raeshkul and Oskar'enlil because their bodies suggest that elements can become thought, law, and living theory. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Fivefold Stillness requires a Panolima to sit within the presence of all five threads and refuse to let any single element dominate the others. Elemental hoarding is taboo: a Panolima who uses their thread to destroy the balance that sustains others is treated as spiritually starving. Ichor-touched Panolima may leak black steam, carry glassy fault-lines in the skin, hear storms before clouds form, or lose control of one element whenever a divine prison nearby hungers. Heritage focus: Stone, soil, pressure, endurance, and grounded elemental force. Parentage: Horundar earth pressure.
races.canonTraits: Heritage/Bloodline: Panolima — Earth Thread. Stone, soil, pressure, endurance, and grounded elemental force.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Increase one ability score by 2 and another ability score by 1, or increase three different ability scores by 1 each.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid. You also count as an Elemental for prerequisites, spells, magical effects, or items that specifically affect elemental creatures.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Elemental Thread. Choose one thread: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, or Aether. Your appearance, diet, movement, rituals, and magical abilities reflect this thread. You gain the corresponding bloodline gift below.; Elemental Communion. You gain proficiency in Nature, Arcana, Survival, or Religion. During a short or long rest in an environment aligned to your element, you may perform communion to gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus + your Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier.; Elemental Cantrip. You know one cantrip tied to your thread: mold earth for Earth, produce flame for Fire, shape water for Water, gust for Air, or prestidigitation for Aether. Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability, chosen when selecting this race.; Harmonic Presence. Your body subtly harmonizes with places aligned to your thread. You can sense hazards, disturbances, and strong elemental currents within 30 feet if you spend 1 minute focusing.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Primordial. Best Classes: Druid, Sorcerer, Cleric, Monk, Ranger, Wizard, Barbarian, or any class reflecting the chosen thread.; Heritage/Bloodline: Panolima — Earth Thread; Theme: Stone, soil, pressure, endurance, and grounded elemental force; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Horundar earth pressure; Inherited Expression: Stone, soil, pressure, endurance, and grounded elemental force
races.dmHook: Use Panolima NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

You are a Humanoid. You also count as an Elemental for prerequisites, spells, magical effects, or items that specifically affect elemental creatures.; Size. You are Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 30 feet.; Elemental Thread. Choose one thread: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, or Aether. Your appearance, diet, movement, rituals, and magical abilities reflect this thread. You gain the corresponding bloodline gift below.; Elemental Communion. You gain proficiency in Nature, Arcana, Survival, or Religion. During a short or long rest in an environment aligned to your element, you may perform communion to gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus + your Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier.; Elemental Cantrip. You know one cantrip tied to your thread: mold earth for Earth, produce flame for Fire, shape water for Water, gust for Air, or prestidigitation for Aether. Constitution, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability, chosen when selecting this race.; Harmonic Presence. Your body subtly harmonizes with places aligned to your thread. You can sense hazards, disturbances, and strong elemental currents within 30 feet if you spend 1 minute focusing.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and Primordial. Best Classes: Druid, Sorcerer, Cleric, Monk, Ranger, Wizard, Barbarian, or any class reflecting the chosen thread.; Heritage/Bloodline: Panolima — Water Thread; Theme: Floods, tides, healing, flow, and pressure; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Horundar and Bastveig water pressure; Inherited Expression: Floods, tides, healing, flow, and pressure

races.name: Panolima — Air Thread
races.categoryDescription: Master of storms, seas, earthquakes, floods, and untamed elemental force.
races.description: Panolima — Air Thread is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Panolima. Theme: Storms, wind, movement, song, and freedom. Parentage / divine pressure: Horundar storm-air pressure. Inherited expression: Storms, wind, movement, song, and freedom. Living Embodiments of Elemental Harmony: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, and Aether. Lore: The Panolimas are beings of elemental harmony . Their bodies are infused with one of the five primordial elemental threads: Earth, Fire, Water, Air, or Aether. Each individual exhibits physical traits reflecting their elemental affinity, from stone-like skin and mossy hair to flowing, water-like forms or luminous, energy-filled auras. They thrive in environments aligned with their element, drawing sustenance, vitality, and energy from landscapes, currents, storms, magma, or ley lines. Their movements, gestures, and even voices often carry the rhythm of their element, and they engage in ritual cleansing or meditation to maintain harmony with their elemental thread. Earth Panolimas have skin patterned like bark or stone, hair resembling moss or soil, and a deep-toned, rumbling voice. They require contact with solid terrain, forests, caves, or mountains to maintain vitality. Their abilities include sensing structural weaknesses, reinforcing the land, and enduring physical hardships with exceptional resilience. They can read terrain, detect mineral-rich soils, and manipulate stone or plant life to shape their surroundings. Fire Panolimas exhibit cracks like magma across their skin, ember-red hair, and glowing eyes. They rely on heat sources, volcanic activity, and exposure to flames to sustain energy. Their bodies radiate warmth and light, and they can manipulate molten materials, summon bursts of flame, and purify areas through fire.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Horundar. Horundar is the primary creator of the Panolima, whose elemental threads of earth, fire, water, air, and aether embody the unstable boundaries of ocean, storm, pressure, and upheaval. Panolima do not merely use elements; they are living negotiations between forces that would otherwise break the world apart. Secondary Divine Pressures. Oskar'enlil studies Panolima balance as a living experiment in civilization, instinct, elemental pressure, and structured survival. Bastveig influences Water Panolima who treat currents and reefs as living memory. Marduthor appears among Fire and Earth Panolima who turn heat, stone, and metal into craft. Sigrananna shapes plant-rich Earth and Air communities through wilderness harmony, while Valkhamesh influences storm-born Panolima who see hierarchy in lightning and pressure. Elemental Influence Notes. Earth Panolima often honor Anubaldir, Marduthor, or Sigrananna depending on whether their culture emphasizes burden, craft, or roots. Fire Panolima may honor Marduthor's forge or Enkirael's battle-flame, creating a major ethical divide. Water Panolima most often answer Horundar and Bastveig together. Air Panolima add Sokhivar for song, prophecy, and sky freedom. Aether Panolima are frequently watched by Raeshkul and Oskar'enlil because their bodies suggest that elements can become thought, law, and living theory. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Fivefold Stillness requires a Panolima to sit within the presence of all five threads and refuse to let any single element dominate the others. Elemental hoarding is taboo: a Panolima who uses their thread to destroy the balance that sustains others is treated as spiritually starving. Ichor-touched Panolima may leak black steam, carry glassy fault-lines in the skin, hear storms before clouds form, or lose control of one element whenever a divine prison nearby hungers. Heritage focus: Storms, wind, movement, song, and freedom. Parentage: Horundar storm-air pressure.

races.name: Jaspey — Mosscap Lineage
races.categoryDescription: Goddess of seasonal rebirth, decay, fungi, roots, and living wilderness.
races.description: Jaspey — Mosscap Lineage is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Jaspey. Theme: Moss, hearth medicine, cozy burrows, and green renewal. Parentage / divine pressure: Hathruna rebirth with Ishtanora hearth pressure. Inherited expression: Moss, hearth medicine, cozy burrows, and green renewal. Ability Score Increase. Lore: Tiny fungal raccoon-folk born from ancient symbiotic spores and scavenger spirits, Jaspey are eerie yet lovable creatures that blur the line between woodland fae, living fungus, and grave-born scavenger. Though their appearance often resembles tiny undead raccoons stitched together with moss, mushrooms, and decay, Jaspey are fully living beings with beating hearts, warm emotions, and vibrant cultures. Standing only a few inches tall, Jaspey can sleep inside coat pockets, teacups, backpacks, hollow books, hat brims, or even small tins. Entire hidden settlements may exist unnoticed inside old walls, cemetery gardens, forgotten cupboards, abandoned wagons, or the roots of ancient trees. Jaspey are natural scavengers, herbalists, healers, thieves, potion brewers, and strange little mystics. They collect shiny objects obsessively, form strong emotional bonds with larger companions, and often travel alongside adventuring parties as medics, alchemists, scouts, or magical nuisances.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Hathruna. Hathruna is the primary creator of the Jaspey, blooming tiny fungal raccoon-folk from rot, spores, roots, scavenger spirits, and the cycle of decay into renewal. Jaspey theology teaches that nothing useful is truly trash: what the powerful discard becomes food, medicine, shelter, story, and sometimes rebellion. Secondary Divine Pressures. Ishtanora is a beloved hearth-patron to Jaspey villages because fungal gardens turn decay into comfort, medicine, and communal survival. Nefarokir is respected in grave-gardens where bones, memory, and compost are carefully separated so the dead are not stolen from their proper passage. Thoryn-Rahek is feared in uncontrolled spore blooms, violent mutations, and fungal hungers that no colony can name. Marduthor is honored by scavenger-crafters who turn broken locks, bottlecaps, wire, and discarded tools into practical magic. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The First Find is a rite where a young Jaspey brings home something abandoned and explains how it can serve the colony. The Soft Burial places beloved dead in protected fungal beds, not to erase them, but to let their last gifts become medicine or shelter. Wasting salvage, poisoning a communal spore-bed, or hoarding medicine during sickness are severe taboos. Ichor-touched Jaspey may grow black-edged mushrooms, dream through nearby fungus, or produce spores that briefly reveal hidden decay, lies, or sickness. DM Hooks. Use Jaspey pantheon influence for undercity colonies, fungal healers, sacred scavenger routes, rot-cults, grave-garden disputes, or tiny heroes who know exactly which forgotten scrap can save a doomed settlement. Heritage focus: Moss, hearth medicine, cozy burrows, and green renewal. Parentage: Hathruna rebirth with Ishtanora hearth pressure.
races.canonTraits: Heritage/Bloodline: Jaspey — Mosscap Lineage. Moss, hearth medicine, cozy burrows, and green renewal.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase.; Your Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Wisdom or Intelligence score increases by 1.; Age.; Jaspey mature rapidly, reaching adulthood around 4 years old. Most live between 25 and 40 years, though ancient fungal sages and colony elders sometimes survive for over a century under ideal conditions.; Alignment.; Jaspey tend toward chaotic or neutral alignments. They value adaptability, freedom, comfort, curiosity, and survival more than laws or rigid traditions.; Most Jaspey are not malicious, though some become selfish scavengers, manipulative tricksters, grave robbers, or unsettling fungal occultists.; Creature Type.; You are a Humanoid.; You are also considered a Plant for any prerequisite, spell, magical effect, or item that specifically affects plant creatures.; Size.; You are Tiny.; Heritage/Bloodline: Jaspey — Mosscap Lineage; Theme: Moss, hearth medicine, cozy burrows, and green renewal; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Hathruna rebirth with Ishtanora hearth pressure; Inherited Expression: Moss, hearth medicine, cozy burrows, and green renewal
races.dmHook: Use Jaspey NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase.; Your Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Wisdom or Intelligence score increases by 1.; Age.; Jaspey mature rapidly, reaching adulthood around 4 years old. Most live between 25 and 40 years, though ancient fungal sages and colony elders sometimes survive for over a century under ideal conditions.; Alignment.; Jaspey tend toward chaotic or neutral alignments. They value adaptability, freedom, comfort, curiosity, and survival more than laws or rigid traditions.; Most Jaspey are not malicious, though some become selfish scavengers, manipulative tricksters, grave robbers, or unsettling fungal occultists.; Creature Type.; You are a Humanoid.; You are also considered a Plant for any prerequisite, spell, magical effect, or item that specifically affects plant creatures.; Size.; You are Tiny.; Heritage/Bloodline: Jaspey — Rotcap Lineage; Theme: Decay, rot-medicine, grave compost, and renewal after ruin; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Hathruna decay and renewal pressure; Inherited Expression: Decay, rot-medicine, grave compost, and renewal after ruin

races.name: Deorbasun — Hunter Instinct
races.categoryDescription: Primordial mother of beasts, fertility, instinct, and natural kinship.
races.description: Deorbasun — Hunter Instinct is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Deorbasun. Theme: Predatory tracking, stealth, pursuit, and survival instinct. Parentage / divine pressure: Iskareth hunter instinct. Inherited expression: Predatory tracking, stealth, pursuit, and survival instinct. Majestic Beast-Souled Guardians of Instinct, Honor, and Mystical Territory. Lore: Deorbasuns are majestic. Their physiques range from muscular, feline-like frames to lithe, agile forms with fur, scales, or earth-toned skin, reflecting a deep connection to predatory grace, wilderness instinct, and mystical energy. Heights vary widely, from six to nine feet, and their limbs are long and flexible, ending in retractable claws or talons capable of channeling energy or venom. Eyes glow with intelligence and authority, displaying hues from ember and gold to crystalline blues, greens, or silver, and often carry faint luminescence that responds to magic or emotion. Hair and manes vary from obsidian black, smoldering crimson, and radiant gold to bioluminescent strands, shimmered locks, or ethereal highlights, often adorned with beads, feathers, bones, or gemstones marking achievements, lineage, or ritual significance. Wings, tails, or subtle soul-animal traits enhance both presence and ability, with wings capable of flight or generating gusts, tails delivering venom, radiant energy, or prehensile utility, and faint ethereal markings or patterns tying form to instinct and magic. Deorbasuns possess extraordinary physical and magical abilities that blend predatory skill, shapeshifting versatility, and environmental attunement. They can leap with precision, maneuver through air or terrain with grace, and strike with claws, fangs, or tails imbued with energy or venom. Some can shift subtly to reflect traits of a soul-animal, such as enhanced senses, agility, or elemental affinities, while others maintain a consistently imposing, feline-like or beastly form. Their endurance and stamina are exceptional, allowing them to traverse rugged terrain, hunt, or perform ritualized movement for extended periods. They absorb energy through magical currents, prey, or ambient mana, and their focus and vitality are renewed through hunting, meditation, ritual exposure, or communion with natural and mystical environments. Deorbasuns are highly perceptive, capable of reading subtle environmental cues, sensing magical disturbances, and coordinating with wildlife or allies in ways that reflect both strategic intelligence and instinctual response.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Iskareth. Iskareth is the primary creator of the Deorbasun, shaping their beast-souled bodies from instinct, fertility, natural kinship, and guardian grace. Their soul-animal traits are understood as divine fragments of Iskareth's first wilderness memory: claw, wing, fang, horn, hide, and watchful eye each represent a sacred duty to defend territory without severing kinship with the living world. Secondary Divine Pressures. Anubaldir is honored by territorial guardians who treat endurance and burden as inherited obligations rather than personal virtues. Asethyr influences revels, hunt-feasts, and tribal rites where wilderness freedom becomes sacred survival. Nebyrr appears in boundary law, marriage bonds between guardian clans, and formal oaths that decide who may pass through protected territory. Sigrananna is invoked by moonlit forest Deorbasun, especially those who guard elven groves, old roads, or sacred beasts. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Boundary Vigil sends young Deorbasun to walk the edge of a chosen territory for one full night without speaking, learning where duty ends and domination begins. The First Hunt is not a kill-trial, but a restraint-trial: the initiate must prove they can track, judge, and spare when the land does not require blood. Wasting prey, abandoning a sworn boundary, or hunting only for vanity are deep taboos. Ichor-touched Deorbasun may show glowing paw-print markings, stone-heavy bones, prophetic animal dreams, or a voice that quiets frightened beasts. DM Hooks. Their divine culture supports shrine guardians, border wardens, sacred beast judges, territorial paladins, cursed exiles, or clans divided between Iskareth's kinship and Enkirael's domination. A Deorbasun antagonist does not need to be evil: they may simply believe the party has crossed a boundary the gods themselves once marked. Heritage focus: Predatory tracking, stealth, pursuit, and survival instinct. Parentage: Iskareth hunter instinct.
races.canonTraits: Heritage/Bloodline: Deorbasun — Hunter Instinct. Predatory tracking, stealth, pursuit, and survival instinct.
races.belavadosAbilities: Ability Score Increase. Your Strength or Dexterity score increases by 2, and your Wisdom or Charisma score increases by 1.; Creature Type. You are a Humanoid.; Size. You are Medium. Many Deorbasuns stand between six and nine feet tall, but your playable size remains Medium.; Speed. Your base walking speed is 35 feet.; Predatory Natural Weapons. Your claws, fangs, talons, horns, or tail are natural weapons. When you hit with an unarmed strike, you may deal slashing, piercing, or bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength or Dexterity modifier.; Soul-Animal Instinct. Choose one instinct when selecting this race: Hunter, Guardian, Winged, Venomtail, or Mystic. Hunter grants proficiency in Stealth or Survival. Guardian grants proficiency in Intimidation or Athletics. Winged grants the Winged Maneuver trait below. Venomtail lets your natural weapon deal poison damage instead of its normal type once per turn. Mystic grants proficiency in Arcana, Nature, or Religion.; Ritual Hunt. Once per long rest, as a bonus action, you mark one creature you can see for 1 minute. The first time each turn you hit the marked creature with an attack, you deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. This damage type matches your natural weapon or a magical energy associated with your soul-animal.; Winged Maneuver. If you chose the Winged instinct, your wings allow brief aerial control. When you jump or fall, you may glide up to 20 feet horizontally and reduce falling damage by an amount equal to five times your level.; Territorial Awareness. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception or Survival) checks made to track creatures, sense intruders, identify disturbed terrain, or notice magical disturbances in your claimed territory or current campsite.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language of your choice. Best Classes: Barbarian, Fighter, Ranger, Druid, Paladin, Monk, or any guardian/hunter role.; Heritage/Bloodline: Deorbasun — Hunter Instinct; Theme: Predatory tracking, stealth, pursuit, and survival instinct; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Iskareth hunter instinct; Inherited Expression: Predatory tracking, stealth, pursuit, and survival instinct
races.dmHook: Use Deorbasun NPCs to show how their creator-god doctrine appears in ancestry, culture, faction standing, rituals, temptation, and divine backlash.

Guardian grants proficiency in Intimidation or Athletics. Winged grants the Winged Maneuver trait below. Venomtail lets your natural weapon deal poison damage instead of its normal type once per turn. Mystic grants proficiency in Arcana, Nature, or Religion.; Ritual Hunt. Once per long rest, as a bonus action, you mark one creature you can see for 1 minute. The first time each turn you hit the marked creature with an attack, you deal extra damage equal to your proficiency bonus. This damage type matches your natural weapon or a magical energy associated with your soul-animal.; Winged Maneuver. If you chose the Winged instinct, your wings allow brief aerial control. When you jump or fall, you may glide up to 20 feet horizontally and reduce falling damage by an amount equal to five times your level.; Territorial Awareness. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception or Survival) checks made to track creatures, sense intruders, identify disturbed terrain, or notice magical disturbances in your claimed territory or current campsite.; Languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and one additional language of your choice. Best Classes: Barbarian, Fighter, Ranger, Druid, Paladin, Monk, or any guardian/hunter role.; Heritage/Bloodline: Deorbasun — Venomtail Instinct; Theme: Venom, deterrence, warning display, and brutal territorial survival; Parentage/Divine Pressure: Iskareth predation with Thoryn-Rahek danger pressure; Inherited Expression: Venom, deterrence, warning display, and brutal territorial survival

races.name: Deorbasun — Mystic Instinct
races.categoryDescription: Primordial mother of beasts, fertility, instinct, and natural kinship.
races.description: Deorbasun — Mystic Instinct is a selectable Belavadös heritage/bloodline option derived from Deorbasun. Theme: Dream-beast signs, sacred visions, and guardian spirit rites. Parentage / divine pressure: Iskareth instinct with Raeshkul/Nefarokir vision pressure. Inherited expression: Dream-beast signs, sacred visions, and guardian spirit rites. Majestic Beast-Souled Guardians of Instinct, Honor, and Mystical Territory. Lore: Deorbasuns are majestic. Their physiques range from muscular, feline-like frames to lithe, agile forms with fur, scales, or earth-toned skin, reflecting a deep connection to predatory grace, wilderness instinct, and mystical energy. Heights vary widely, from six to nine feet, and their limbs are long and flexible, ending in retractable claws or talons capable of channeling energy or venom. Eyes glow with intelligence and authority, displaying hues from ember and gold to crystalline blues, greens, or silver, and often carry faint luminescence that responds to magic or emotion. Hair and manes vary from obsidian black, smoldering crimson, and radiant gold to bioluminescent strands, shimmered locks, or ethereal highlights, often adorned with beads, feathers, bones, or gemstones marking achievements, lineage, or ritual significance. Wings, tails, or subtle soul-animal traits enhance both presence and ability, with wings capable of flight or generating gusts, tails delivering venom, radiant energy, or prehensile utility, and faint ethereal markings or patterns tying form to instinct and magic. Deorbasuns possess extraordinary physical and magical abilities that blend predatory skill, shapeshifting versatility, and environmental attunement. They can leap with precision, maneuver through air or terrain with grace, and strike with claws, fangs, or tails imbued with energy or venom. Some can shift subtly to reflect traits of a soul-animal, such as enhanced senses, agility, or elemental affinities, while others maintain a consistently imposing, feline-like or beastly form. Their endurance and stamina are exceptional, allowing them to traverse rugged terrain, hunt, or perform ritualized movement for extended periods. They absorb energy through magical currents, prey, or ambient mana, and their focus and vitality are renewed through hunting, meditation, ritual exposure, or communion with natural and mystical environments. Deorbasuns are highly perceptive, capable of reading subtle environmental cues, sensing magical disturbances, and coordinating with wildlife or allies in ways that reflect both strategic intelligence and instinctual response.
races.pantheonInfluence: Creator God: Iskareth. Iskareth is the primary creator of the Deorbasun, shaping their beast-souled bodies from instinct, fertility, natural kinship, and guardian grace. Their soul-animal traits are understood as divine fragments of Iskareth's first wilderness memory: claw, wing, fang, horn, hide, and watchful eye each represent a sacred duty to defend territory without severing kinship with the living world. Secondary Divine Pressures. Anubaldir is honored by territorial guardians who treat endurance and burden as inherited obligations rather than personal virtues. Asethyr influences revels, hunt-feasts, and tribal rites where wilderness freedom becomes sacred survival. Nebyrr appears in boundary law, marriage bonds between guardian clans, and formal oaths that decide who may pass through protected territory. Sigrananna is invoked by moonlit forest Deorbasun, especially those who guard elven groves, old roads, or sacred beasts. Rites, Taboos, and Ichor Marks. The Boundary Vigil sends young Deorbasun to walk the edge of a chosen territory for one full night without speaking, learning where duty ends and domination begins. The First Hunt is not a kill-trial, but a restraint-trial: the initiate must prove they can track, judge, and spare when the land does not require blood. Wasting prey, abandoning a sworn boundary, or hunting only for vanity are deep taboos. Ichor-touched Deorbasun may show glowing paw-print markings, stone-heavy bones, prophetic animal dreams, or a voice that quiets frightened beasts. DM Hooks. Their divine culture supports shrine guardians, border wardens, sacred beast judges, territorial paladins, cursed exiles, or clans divided between Iskareth's kinship and Enkirael's domination. A Deorbasun antagonist does not need to be evil: they may simply believe the party has crossed a boundary the gods themselves once marked. Heritage focus: Dream-beast signs, sacred visions, and guardian spirit rites. Parentage: Iskareth instinct with Raeshkul/Nefarokir vision pressure.

Your claws, fangs, talons, horns, or tail are natural weapons. categories.description: Creator of civilization, lineage, dynasties, marriage, and social order.
Mistress of forests, moonlight, wilderness, and graceful hunters.
Divine smith and architect of invention, craft, metalwork, and underground halls.
Patron of hearths, harvests, humble villages, and peaceful rural life.
Born from conflict, conquest, fury, survival, and martial dominance.
Titan of endurance, burden, colossal strength, and primal might.
Creator of thunder-blooded sovereign races tied to ancient power and domination.
Mistress of crossroads, curses, planar mysteries, and supernatural bloodlines.
Lord of death, the underworld, spirits, and the forgotten dead.
Patron of tricksters, shapeshifters, wanderers, dreamers, and magical mischief.
Master of storms, seas, earthquakes, floods, and untamed elemental force.
Goddess of intellect, strategy, psionic discipline, and higher understanding.
Shaper of artificial life, stolen knowledge, innovation, and awakened constructs.
Sky-soaring creator tied to sunlight, prophecy, music, and divine freedom.
Primordial mother of beasts, fertility, instinct, and natural kinship.
Ancient mother of tides, reefs, hidden waters, and oceanic civilizations.
Creator of ancient malformed beings born from primordial chaos and forgotten ages.
Goddess of seasonal rebirth, decay, fungi, roots, and living wilderness.
Creator of cursed shadows, transformations, forbidden magic, and eerie twilight races.
Shaped experimental peoples to test balance between civilization, instinct, and adaptation.
God of primal freedom, tribal rites, revelry, instinct, and untamed cultures.
Created rare and enigmatic peoples defined by uniqueness, beauty, charisma, and emotional influence.

belavadosAlignments.name: Lawful Coordinator
